Uses §13.11 multi-search for results + facets; §13.6 session pinning for read-your-writes if the host app is doing concurrent writes; §13.10 query coalescing for efficient instant-search.\n\n**Config.**\n```yaml\nsearch_ui:\n  enabled: true\n  path: /ui/search\n  widget_script_enabled: true\n  embeddable: true\n\n  auth:\n    mode: public                    # public | shared_key | oauth_proxy\n    shared_key_env: \"\"              # env var holding the shared key (shared_key mode)\n    session_ttl_s: 900\n    session_rate_limit: \"10/minute\"\n    jwt_secret_env: SEARCH_UI_JWT_SECRET\n    oauth_proxy:\n      user_header: X-Forwarded-User\n      groups_header: X-Forwarded-Groups\n      filter_template: \"tenant IN [{groups}]\"   # null = disable filter injection\n      attribute_map:                            # optional per-claim binding\n        groups: groups_array\n        user: user_id_string\n\n  allowed_origins: [\"*\"]\n  scoped_key_max_age_days: 60                  # hard expiry on the scoped Meilisearch key (upper bound on key lifetime)\n  scoped_key_rotate_before_expiry_days: 30     # rotate this many days before the key's expiry (must be strictly less than scoped_key_max_age_days; see \"Config validation\" below)\n  scoped_key_rotation_drain_s: 120     # wait this long after the new generation is in place before revoking the previous key; see \"Revocation safety gate\" above\n\n  rate_limit:\n    per_ip: \"60/minute\"\n    backend: redis                  # redis | local (per-pod); redis required when replicas > 1\n    redis_key_prefix: \"miroir:ratelimit:searchui:\"\n    redis_ttl_s: 60\n\n  cors_allowed_origins: []          # empty = same-origin only\n  csp_overrides:\n    script_src: []                  # additional script-src domains\n    img_src: []                     # additional img-src domains\n    connect_src: []                 # additional connect-src for XHR targets\n  csp: \"default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'\"   # base template; any csp_overrides.* lists are merged into the corresponding directives at render time\n\n  analytics:\n    enabled: false\n    sink: cdc                     # publishes click-throughs as CDC events\n```\n\nWith `rate_limit.backend: local` and multi-pod deployments, the effective per-IP rate is `per_ip × pod_count` per minute cluster-wide (each pod maintains its own bucket table). For production deployments with `miroir.replicas > 1`, the Helm chart's `values.schema.json` rejects `backend: local` at install time, forcing Redis-backed shared state. The Redis bucket keys live under `redis_key_prefix` with `redis_ttl_s` TTL so memory stays bounded even under scan/spray attacks.\n\n**Filter injection in `oauth_proxy` mode.** When `search_ui.auth.oauth_proxy.filter_template` is non-null, Miroir automatically injects the rendered filter into every `/indexes/{uid}/search` call made under the session, using Meilisearch filter-concatenation semantics (the rendered clause is ANDed with any user-supplied filter the SPA sends — `(<user filter>) AND (<injected filter>)`). The `{groups}` placeholder is replaced at session-mint time with the comma-joined values of the `groups_header` (properly JSON-array-escaped for the Meilisearch filter DSL, e.g. `[\"engineering\",\"ops\"]`); the rendered claim is baked into the JWT so every subsequent search under that session enforces the same filter without re-reading proxy headers. `{user}` is replaced with the `user_header` value. `attribute_map` lets operators rename the JWT claim keys exposed to observability tooling and to the client (read-only — the SPA cannot override them). When `filter_template` is null, Miroir performs no filter injection — operators relying on a reverse proxy or downstream filter-at-app layer for access control must enforce it themselves; Miroir still forwards the user/group claims as read-only JWT claims for observability but does not gate results on them.\n\n**Metrics.** `miroir_search_ui_sessions_total`, `miroir_search_ui_queries_total{index}`, `miroir_search_ui_zero_hits_total{index}`, `miroir_search_ui_click_through_total{index}` (when analytics enabled), `miroir_search_ui_p95_ms{index}` (end-user-observed latency, measured via client beacon).\n\n---\n\n## 14. Resource Envelope and Horizontal Scaling\n\nMiroir is designed to fit within a fixed per-pod resource envelope. If aggregate workload exceeds what one pod can serve, Miroir scales **out**, not up — the orchestrator is stateless on the request path and partitions background work across pods by explicit design.\n\n### 14.1 Target envelope\n\nEach orchestrator pod is sized for:\n\n- **2 vCPU**\n- **3.75 GB RAM**\n\nThis matches common small-instance tiers (AWS t3.medium, GCP e2-medium, Hetzner CX22, Rackspace Spot 2c/3.75GB). Meilisearch nodes are sized independently per Section 6 and are unaffected by this envelope.\n\n### 14.2 Per-pod memory budget\n\nSteady-state allocation at default config, assuming all advanced capabilities enabled:\n\n| Component | Budget | Primary knob |\n|-----------|--------|--------------|\n| Rust/Tokio runtime + axum baseline | 80 MB | — |\n| HTTP/2 connection pools to nodes | 50 MB | `connection_pool_per_node` |\n| Request/response buffers (p99 concurrent) | 200 MB | `server.max_body_bytes`, `max_concurrent_requests` |\n| Task registry cache | 100 MB | `task_registry.cache_size` |\n| Idempotency cache (§13.10) | 100 MB | `idempotency.max_cached_keys` |\n| Session pinning state (§13.6) | 50 MB | `session_pinning.max_sessions` |\n| Query coalescing in-flight (§13.10) | 50 MB | `query_coalescing.max_subscribers` |\n| Router + EWMA state (§13.3) | 20 MB | fixed per node |\n| Query plan cache (§13.4) | 20 MB | fixed |\n| Alias table (§13.7) | 10 MB | fixed |\n| Metrics registry (prometheus) | 50 MB | fixed |\n| Dump import buffer (§13.9, only while importing) | 128 MB | `dump_import.memory_buffer_bytes` |\n| Anti-entropy fingerprinter (§13.8, only during pass) | 128 MB | `anti_entropy.max_read_concurrency` × batch |\n| Multi-search batch state (§13.11) | ~5 MB | `multi_search.max_queries_per_batch` (per-request cache) |","numLines":600,"startLine":2817,"totalLines":3753}}}

cursors: HashMap::new(),\n            buffered_count: 0,\n            dropped_count: 0,\n            published_count: 0,\n        }));\n\n        if config.enabled {\n            // Spawn background ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) — plan §13.17.\n//!\n//! Manages rolling time-series indexes with automatic rollover and retention.\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{info, error};\n\n/// ILM rollover policy configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverPolicy {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Write alias name.\n    pub write_alias: String,\n    /// Read alias name (multi-target).\n    pub read_alias: String,\n    /// Index name pattern with {YYYY-MM-DD} placeholder.\n    pub pattern: String,\n    /// Rollover triggers.\n    pub triggers: RolloverTriggers,\n    /// Retention policy.\n    pub retention: RetentionPolicy,\n    /// Index template reference.\n    pub index_template: IndexTemplate,\n}\n\n/// Triggers that cause a rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverTriggers {\n    /// Maximum documents before rollover.\n    pub max_docs: u64,\n    /// Maximum age before rollover (e.g., \"7d\").\n    pub max_age: String,\n    /// Maximum index size before rollover (GB).\n    pub max_size_gb: u32,\n}\n\n/// Retention policy for old indexes.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RetentionPolicy {\n    /// Number of indexes to keep.\n    pub keep_indexes: u32,\n}\n\n/// Index template for rollover.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IndexTemplate {\n    /// Primary key field.\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    /// Named settings profile reference.\n    pub settings_ref: String,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmState {\n    /// Registered policies.\n    pub policies: Vec<RolloverPolicy>,\n    /// Active rollover operations.\n    pub active_rollovers: HashMap<String, RolloverOperation>,\n    /// Last check timestamp (UNIX ms).\n    pub last_check_ms: u64,\n}\n\n/// Active rollover operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RolloverOperation {\n    /// Policy name.\n    pub policy_name: String,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: RolloverPhase,\n    /// New index UID.\n    pub new_index: String,\n    /// Old index UID.\n    pub old_index: String,\n    /// Started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    /// Error message if failed.\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Rollover phase.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum RolloverPhase {\n    Creating,\n    FlippingAlias,\n    UpdatingReadAlias,\n    CleaningOld,\n    Complete,\n    Failed,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager — handles index lifecycle for time-series data.\npub struct IlmManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: IlmConfig,\n    /// Shared state.\n    state: Arc<RwLock<IlmState>>,\n}\n\n/// ILM manager configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IlmConfig {\n    /// Whether ILM is enabled.\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Check interval (seconds).\n    pub check_interval_s: u64,\n    /// Safety lock: refuse to delete indexes newer than this (days).\n    pub safety_lock_older_than_days: u32,\n    /// Maximum rollovers per check.\n    pub max_rollovers_per_check: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for IlmConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            check_interval_s: 3600,\n            safety_lock_older_than_days: 7,\n            max_rollovers_per_check: 10,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl IlmManager {\n    /// Create a new ILM manager.\n    pub fn new(config: IlmConfig) -> Self {\n        let state = Arc::new(RwLock::new(IlmState {\n            policies: Vec::new(),\n            active_rollovers: HashMap::new(),\n            last_check_ms: 0,\n        }));\n\n        if config.enabled {\n            // Spawn background evaluator\n            let state_clone = state.clone();\n            let config_clone = config.clone();\n            tokio::spawn(async move {\n                Self::background_evaluator(state_clone, config_clone).await;\n            });\n        }\n\n        Self { config, state }\n    }\n\n    /// Register a rollover policy.\n    pub async fn register_policy(&self, policy: RolloverPolicy) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.push(policy);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Unregister a policy.\n    pub async fn unregister_policy(&self, name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.policies.retain(|p| p.name != name);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get all policies.\n    pub async fn policies(&self) -> Vec<RolloverPolicy> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.policies.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get active rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn active_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Option<RolloverOperation> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.get(policy_name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Trigger an immediate rollover for a policy.\n    pub async fn trigger_rollover(&self, policy_name: &str) -> Result<(), IlmError> {\n        let state = self.state.read().await;\n        let policy = state.policies.iter()\n            .find(|p| p.name == policy_name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| IlmError::PolicyNotFound(policy_name.to_string()))?;\n\n        // Create rollover operation\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let new_index = Self::format_index_name(&policy.pattern, now);\n        let operation = RolloverOperation {\n            policy_name: policy_name.to_string(),\n            phase: RolloverPhase::Creating,\n            new_index: new_index.clone(),\n            old_index: format!(\"{}-current\", policy.write_alias),\n            started_at: now,\n            error: None,\n        };\n\n        drop(state);\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        state.active_rollovers.insert(policy_name.to_string(), operation);\n\n        info!(\"ILM: triggered rollover for policy '{}', new index: {}\", policy_name, new_index);\n        Ok(())\n    }","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":439}}}

\"default_ewma_half_life\")]\n    pub ewma_half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Exploration probability (epsilon).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_epsilon\")]\n    pub exploration_epsilon: f64,\n}\n\nfn default_error_weight() -> f64 {\n    10.0\n}\nfn default_ewma_half_life() -> u64 {\n    5000\n}\nfn default_epsilon() -> f64 {\n    0.05\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    fn Hedged requests for tail-latency mitigation (plan §13.2).\n//!\n//! Issues duplicate requests to alternate replicas when the primary exceeds\n//! its p95 latency, returning the first response and cancelling the slower.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Hedging configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct HedgingConfig {\n    /// Whether hedging is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Hedge at p95_trigger_multiplier × observed p95.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_trigger_multiplier\")]\n    pub p95_trigger_multiplier: f64,\n    /// Never hedge sooner than this (ms).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_min_trigger_ms\")]\n    pub min_trigger_ms: u64,\n    /// Maximum hedges per query.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_hedges\")]\n    pub max_hedges_per_query: usize,\n    /// Allow falling back to another replica group.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub cross_group_fallback: bool,\n}\n\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_trigger_multiplier() -> f64 {\n    1.2\n}\nfn default_min_trigger_ms() -> u64 {\n    15\n}\nfn default_max_hedges() -> usize {\n    2\n}\n\nimpl Default for HedgingConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            p95_trigger_multiplier: default_trigger_multiplier(),\n            min_trigger_ms: default_min_trigger_ms(),\n            max_hedges_per_query: default_max_hedges(),\n            cross_group_fallback: true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Per-node latency tracking for hedging decisions.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeLatencyStats {\n    /// Node ID.\n    pub node_id: String,\n    /// EWMA of p95 latency (ms).\n    pub p95_ms: f64,\n    /// In-flight request count.\n    pub in_flight: u32,\n    /// Error rate (EWMA).\n    pub error_rate: f64,\n}\n\nimpl NodeLatencyStats {\n    pub fn new(node_id: String) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            node_id,\n            p95_ms: 100.0, // Initial estimate\n            in_flight: 0,\n            error_rate: 0.0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update with a new latency sample.\n    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n        // EWMA with alpha = 0.1 (half-life ~7 samples)\n        const ALPHA: f64 = 0.1;\n        self.p95_ms = ALPHA * latency_ms + (1.0 - ALPHA) * self.p95_ms;\n    }\n\n    /// Record an error.\n    pub fn record_error(&mut self) {\n        const ERROR_ALPHA: f64 = 0.05;\n        self.error_rate = ERROR_ALPHA + (1.0 - ERROR_ALPHA) * self.error_rate;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a success.\n    pub fn record_success(&mut self) {\n        const ERROR_DECAY: f64 = 0.1;\n        self.error_rate = (1.0 - ERROR_DECAY) * self.error_rate;\n    }\n\n    /// Increment in-flight count.\n    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hedging manager.\npub struct HedgingManager {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: HedgingConfig,\n    /// Per-node latency stats.\n    node_stats: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, NodeLatencyStats>>>,\n}\n\nimpl HedgingManager {\n    /// Create a new hedging manager.\n    pub fn new(config: HedgingConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            node_stats: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get or create stats for a node.\n    pub async fn get_or_create_stats(&self, node_id: &str) -> NodeLatencyStats {\n        let mut stats = self.node_stats.write().await;\n        stats.entry(node_id.to_string())\n            .or_insert_with(|| NodeLatencyStats::new(node_id.to_string()))\n            .clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Record a request completion.\n    pub async fn record_completion(&self, node_id: &str, latency_ms: f64, success: bool) {\n        let mut stats = self.node_stats.write().await;\n        let entry = stats.entry(node_id.to_string())\n            .or_insert_with(|| NodeLatencyStats::new(node_id.to_string()));\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n        if success {\n            entry.record_success();\n        } else {\n            entry.record_error();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate the hedge deadline for a node.","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":332}}}

Atomic index aliases for blue-green reindexing (plan §13.7).\n//!\n//! This module implements the alias layer that allows atomic index flips\n//! without downtime. Aliases resolve to one or more concrete Meilisearch\n//! index UIDs, supporting both single-target (writable) and multi-target\n//! (read-only, used by ILM) aliases.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Alias kind: single-target (writable) or multi-target (read-only, ILM-managed).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"lowercase\")]\npub enum AliasKind {\n    Single,\n    Multi,\n}\n\n/// A single alias record from the task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Alias {\n    /// Alias name (the key clients use).\n    pub name: String,\n    /// `single` or `multi`.\n    pub kind: AliasKind,\n    /// Current target UID (only set when kind=single).\n    pub current_uid: Option<String>,\n    /// Target UIDs as JSON array (only set when kind=multi).\n    pub target_uids: Option<Vec<String>>,\n    /// Generation incremented on each flip.\n    pub generation: u64,\n    /// Created at timestamp.\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Last updated timestamp.\n    pub updated_at: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Alias {\n    /// Create a new single-target alias.\n    pub fn new_single(name: String, target_uid: String) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Single,\n            current_uid: Some(target_uid),\n            target_uids: None,\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new multi-target alias.\n    pub fn new_multi(name: String, target_uids: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Self {\n            name,\n            kind: AliasKind::Multi,\n            current_uid: None,\n            target_uids: Some(target_uids),\n            generation: 0,\n            created_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the effective target UIDs for this alias.\n    pub fn targets(&self) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        match self.kind {\n            AliasKind::Single => {\n                let uid = self.current_uid.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"single alias missing current_uid\".into()))?;\n                Ok(vec![uid.clone()])\n            }\n            AliasKind::Multi => {\n                let uids = self.target_uids.as_ref()\n                    .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"multi alias missing target_uids\".into()))?;\n                Ok(uids.clone())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Flip this alias to a new target (single-target only).\n    pub fn flip(&mut self, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Single {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot flip multi-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.current_uid = Some(new_target);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update multi-target alias UIDs (ILM-only).\n    pub fn update_targets(&mut self, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.kind != AliasKind::Multi {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"cannot update_targets on single-target alias\".into()));\n        }\n        self.target_uids = Some(new_targets);\n        self.generation += 1;\n        self.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap_or_default()\n            .as_secs();\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// In-memory alias registry with task-store persistence.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AliasRegistry {\n    aliases: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, Alias>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AliasRegistry {\n    /// Create a new empty alias registry.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            aliases: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Resolve an index UID or alias name to concrete target UIDs.\n    ///\n    /// If `input` is not a known alias, returns it as-is (treat as concrete UID).\n    pub async fn resolve(&self, input: &str) -> Vec<String> {\n        let aliases = self.aliases.read().await;\n        match aliases.get(input) {\n            Some(alias) => alias.targets().unwrap_or_else(|_| vec![input.to_string()]),\n            None => vec![input.to_string()],\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an input is an alias (vs a concrete UID).\n    pub async fn is_alias(&self, input: &str) -> bool {\n        self.aliases.read().await.contains_key(input)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a single alias by name.\n    pub async fn get(&self, name: &str) -> Option<Alias> {\n        self.aliases.read().await.get(name).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// List all aliases.\n    pub async fn list(&self) -> Vec<Alias> {\n        self.aliases.read().await.values().cloned().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Create or update an alias.\n    pub async fn upsert(&self, alias: Alias) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        aliases.insert(alias.name.clone(), alias);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Delete an alias.\n    pub async fn delete(&self, name: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        Ok(aliases.remove(name).is_some())\n    }\n\n    /// Flip a single-target alias atomically.\n    pub async fn flip(&self, name: &str, new_target: String) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n        alias.flip(new_target)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Update a multi-target alias (ILM use only).\n    pub async fn update_multi(&self, name: &str, new_targets: Vec<String>) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut aliases = self.aliases.write().await;\n        let alias = aliases.get_mut(name)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::NotFound(format!(\"alias '{}' not found\", name)))?;\n        alias.update_targets(new_targets)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for AliasRegistry {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {","numLines":200,"startLine":1,"totalLines":289}}}

PreflightResponse {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalTermStats {\n    pub df: u64,\n    pub idf: f64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct GlobalIdf {\n    pub total_docs: u64,\n    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n}\n\nimpl GlobalIdf {\n    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n        for resp in responses {\n            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n            total_length / total_docs as f64\n        } else {\n            0.0\n        };\n\n        let n = total_docs as f64;\n        let terms = term_df\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(term, df)| {\n                let idf = if df == 0 {\n                    0.0\n                } else {\n                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n                };\n                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// NodeClient trait\n// Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n///\n/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\npub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n    h.finish()\n}\n\n/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n///\n/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n///\n/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n/// for deterministic resolution.\npub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n        .iter()\n        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n        .collect();\n    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n    });\n    scored\n        .into_iter()\n        .take(rf)\n        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\npub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n    topology\n        .groups()\n        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n///\n/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\npub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n    if replica_groups == 0 {\n        return 0;\n    }\n    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\npub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n        })\n        .collect::<std::collections::HashSet<_>>()\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect()\n}\n\n/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\npub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n}\n\n/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n///\n/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n/// For each shard, counts nodes in new assignment that weren't in old.\npub fn count_assignment_diff(\n    old_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n    new_shards: &[(u32, Vec<NodeId>)],\n) -> usize {\n    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n        .iter()\n        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut diff = 0;\n    for (shard, new_nodes) in new_shards {\n        if let Some(old_nodes) = old_map.get(shard) {\n            // Count nodes in new that weren't in old (counts each change once)\n            for node in new_nodes {\n                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n                    diff += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    diff\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    /// Test 1: Determinism — same inputs always produce the same output.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_determinism() {\n        let nodes = vec![\n            NodeId::new(\"node-a\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-b\".to_string()),\n            NodeId::new(\"node-c\".to_string()),\n        ];\n\n        let reference = (0..100)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)))\n (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_3, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let new_assignment: Vec<_> = (0..shard_count)\n            .map(|shard_id| (shard_id, assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes_4, rf)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let diff = count_assignment_diff(&old_assignment, &new_assignment);\n        let max_diff = 2 * (shard_count as f64 / 4.0).ceil() as usize;\n\n        assert!(\n            diff <= max_diff,\n            \"Add reshuffle exceeded bound: {} > {}\",\n            diff,\n            max_diff\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test 3: Reshuffle bound on remove — 64 shards, 4→3 nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Expected: ~RF × S / Ng edges differ.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_reshuffle_bound_on_remove() {\n return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n151\t                window: raw.clone(),\n152\t            };\n153\t        }\n154\t    }\n155\t\n156\t    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n157\t        utc_now: NoRestriction,\n    /// Current time is outside all allowed windows.\n    Denied {\n        utc_now: String,\n        allowed: Vec<String>,\n    },\n}\n\n/// Check whether resharding is allowed at the check_window(utc_minute: u16, config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    if config.allowed_windows.is_empty() {\n        return WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction;\n    }\n\n    for raw in &config.allowed_windows {\n        let window = match TimeWindow::parse(raw) {\n            Ok(w) => w,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n        if window.contains(utc_minute) {\n            return WindowGuardResult::Allowed {\n                window: raw.clone(),\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    WindowGuardResult::Denied {\n        utc_now: format!(\"{:02}:{:02} UTC\", utc_minute / 60, utc_minute % 60),\n        allowed: config.allowed_windows.clone(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Check the schedule window against the system clock.\npub fn check_window_now(config: &ReshardingConfig) -> WindowGuardResult {\n    let utc_minute = current_utc_minute();\n    check_window(utc_minute, config)\n}\n\nfn current_utc_minute() -> u16 {\n    let duration = storage/write load. Document keys are synthesized for the corpus size\n/// and routed through the real hash function to measure distribution.\npub fn simulate(params: &SimParams) -> SimResult {\n    let total_docs = params.corpus_size_bytes / params.doc_size_bytes;\n    let rf = params.replication_factor;\n    let rg = params.replica_groups;\n    let nodes_per_group = params.nodes_per_group;\n\n    // Build a synthetic topology for the simulation.\n    let groups: Vec<Group> = (0..rg)\n        .map(|g| {\n            let mut group = Group::new(g);\n            for n in 0..nodes_per_group {\n                group.add_node(NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g{}-n{}\", g, n)));\n            }\n            group\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Simulate document distribution across old and new shard counts.\n    // Use the actual router hash to get realistic distribution.\n    let mut old_shard_counts: Vec<u64> = vec![0; replication_factor: 1,\n537\t            old_shards: 64,\n538\t            new_shards: 128,\n539\t            nodes_per_group: 3,\n540\t            backfill_throttle_dps: 10_000,\n541\t        };\n542\t        let result = simulate(&params);\n543\t        assert!(\n544\t            (result.storage_amplification - 2.0).abs() < 0.01,\n545\t            \"expected ~2.0, got {}\",\n546\t            result.storage_amplification\n547\t        );\n548\t    }\n549\t\n550\t    #[test]\n551\t    fn simulation_dual_write_is_2x() {\n552\t        let params = SimParams {\n553\t be very low (< 5%).\n574\t        let params = SimParams {\n575\t            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n576\t            corpus_size_bytes: 1_000_000 * 1024, // 1M docs × 1KB\n577\t }\n621\t\n622\t    #[test]\n623\t    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n624\t        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n625\t        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), cv_empty_and_zero() {\n648\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n649\t        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n650\t    }\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n654\t// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n655\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n656\t\n657\t/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n658\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n659\t#[repr(u8)]\n660\tpub enum ReshardPhase {\n661\t    /// No active resharding.\n662\t    Idle = 0,\n663\t    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n664\t    ShadowCreated = 1,\n665\t    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n666\t    DualWriteActive = 2,\n667\t    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n668\t    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n669\t    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n670\t    Verifying = 4,\n671\t    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n672\t    Swapped = 5,\n673\t    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n674\t    CleaningUp = 6,\n675\t    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n676\t    Complete = 7,\n677\t    /// Resharding failed.\n678\t    Failed = 8,\n679\t}\n680\t\n681\timpl ReshardPhase {\n682\t    /// Human-readable phase name.\n683\t    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n684\t        match self {\n685\t            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n686\t            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n687\t            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n688\t            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n689\t            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n690\t            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n691\t            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n692\t            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n693\t            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n694\t        }\n695\t    }\n696\t\n697\t    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n698\t    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n699\t        match v {\n700\t            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n701\t            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n702\t            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n703\t            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n704\t            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n705\t            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n706\t            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n707\t            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n708\t            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n709\t            _ => None,\n710\t        }\n711\t    }\n712\t}\n713\t\n714\t/// Active resharding operation state.\n715\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n716\tpub struct ReshardOperation {\n717\t    /// Unique operation ID.\n718\t    pub id: String,\n719\t    /// Index UID being resharded.\n720\t    pub index_uid: String,\n721\t    /// Old shard count.\n722\t    pub old_shards: u32,\n723\t    /// New shard count.\n724\t    pub target_shards: u32,\n725\t    /// Current phase.\n726\t    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n727\t    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n728\t    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n729\t    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n730\t    pub created_at: u64,\n731\t    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n732\t    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n733\t    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n734\t    pub total_documents: u64,\n735\t    /// Last error message (if any).\n736\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n737\t    /// Shadow index UID.\n738\t    pub shadow_index: String,\n739\t    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n740\t    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n741\t    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n742\t    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n743\t}\n744\t\n745\t/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n746\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n747\tpub struct VerificationResults {\n748\t    /// Live index PK set size.\n749\t    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n750\t    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n751\t    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n752\t    /// PKs only in live index.\n753\t    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n754\t    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n755\t    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n756\t    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n757\t    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n758\t    /// Whether verification passed.\n759\t    pub passed: bool,\n760\t}\n761\t\n762\timpl ReshardOperation {\n763\t    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n764\t    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n765\t        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n766\t        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n767\t        let now = millis_now();\n768\t        Self {\n769\t            id,\n770\t            index_uid,\n771\t            old_shards,\n772\t            target_shards,\n773\t            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n774\t            phase_started_at: now,\n775\t            created_at: now,\n776\t            documents_backfilled: 0,\n777\t            total_documents: 0,\n778\t            last_error: None,\n779\t            shadow_index,\n780\t            verification_results: None,\n781\t            cleanup_deadline: None,\n782\t        }\n783\t    }\n784\t\n785\t    /// Transition to the next phase.\n786\t    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n787\t        self.phase = new_phase;\n788\t        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n789\t    }\n790\t\n791\t    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n792\t    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n793\t        self.last_error = Some(error);\n794\t        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n795\t    }\n796\t\n797\t    /// Update backfill progress.\n798\t    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n799\t        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n800\t        self.total_documents = total;\n801\t    }\n802\t\n803\t    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n804\t    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n805\t        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n806\t            return 0.0;\n807\t        }\n808\t        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n812\t    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n813\t        matches!(\n814\t            self.phase,\n815\t            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n816\t        )\n817\t    }\n818\t\n819\t    /// Get the shadow index name.\n820\t    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n821\t        &self.shadow_index\n822\t    }\n823\t}\n824\t\n825\t/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\n826\tfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n827\t    SystemTime::now()\n828\t        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n829\t        .unwrap_or_default()\n830\t        .as_millis() as u64\n831\t}\n832\t\n833\t/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n834\t///\n835\t/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n836\t/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n837\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n838\tpub struct ReshardRegistry {\n839\t    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n840\t    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n841\t    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\timpl ReshardRegistry {\n845\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n846\t        Self::default()\n847\t    }\n848\t\n849\t    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n850\t    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n851\t        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n852\t            return Err(format!(\n853\t                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n854\t                op.index_uid, existing_id\n855\t            ));\n856\t        }\n857\t        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n858\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n859\t        Ok(())\n860\t    }\n861\t\n862\t    /// Get an operation by ID.\n863\t    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n864\t        self.operations.get(id)\n865\t    }\n866\t\n867\t    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n868\t    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n869\t        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n870\t    }\n871\t\n872\t    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n873\t    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n874\t        self.index_ops\n875\t            .get(index_uid)\n876\t            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n877\t    }\n878\t\n879\t    /// Update an operation.\n880\t    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n881\t        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n882\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n883\t        }\n884\t        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n885\t        Ok(())\n886\t    }\n887\t\n888\t    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n889\t    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n890\t        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n891\t        if !op.is_terminal() {\n892\t            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n893\t        }\n894\t        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n895\t        Ok(())\n896\t    }\n897\t\n898\t    /// List all operations.\n899\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n900\t        self.operations.values().collect()\n901\t    }\n902\t\n903\t    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n904\t    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n905\t        let now = millis_now();\n906\t        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n907\t        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n908\t            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n909\t                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n910\t                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n911\t            }\n912\t        }\n913\t        for id in to_remove {\n914\t            self.operations.remove(&id);\n915\t        }\n916\t    }\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t#[cfg(test)]\n920\tmod tests_reshard_execution {\n921\t    use TimeWindow::parse(\"ab:00-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid hour\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn time_window_parse_invalid_minute() {\n        let err = TimeWindow::parse(\"02:ab-06:00\").unwrap_err();\n        assert!(err.contains(\"invalid minute\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn check_window_now_no_restriction() {\n        let config = ReshardingConfig::default();\n        assert!(matches!(check_window_now(&config), WindowGuardResult::NoRestriction));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn simulation_throttle_zero_gives_infinity_duration() {\n        let params = SimParams {\n            doc_size_bytes: 1024,\n            corpus_size_bytes: 1024 * 1024,\n            write_rate_dps: 100,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            old_shards: 4,\n            new_shards: 8,\n            nodes_per_group: 2,\n            backfill_throttle_dps: 0,\n        };\n        let result = simulate(&params);\n        assert!(result.backfill_duration_secs.is_infinite());\n        assert_eq!(result.total_bytes_written, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn cv_empty_and_zero() {\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[]), 0.0);\n        assert_eq!(cv(&[0, 0, 0]), 0.0);\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Six-phase resharding execution (plan §13.1)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Resharding phase identifiers (matching plan §13.1 steps).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[repr(u8)]\npub enum ReshardPhase {\n    /// No active resharding.\n    Idle = 0,\n    /// Phase 1: Shadow index created.\n    ShadowCreated = 1,\n    /// Phase 2: Dual-hash dual-write active.\n    DualWriteActive = 2,\n    /// Phase 3: Backfill in progress.\n    BackfillInProgress = 3,\n    /// Phase 4: Verification in progress.\n    Verifying = 4,\n    /// Phase 5: Alias swap completed.\n    Swapped = 5,\n    /// Phase 6: Cleanup in progress.\n    CleaningUp = 6,\n    /// Resharding completed successfully.\n    Complete = 7,\n    /// Resharding failed.\n    Failed = 8,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardPhase {\n    /// Human-readable phase name.\n    pub fn name(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Idle => \"Idle\",\n            Self::ShadowCreated => \"Shadow Created\",\n            Self::DualWriteActive => \"Dual-Write Active\",\n            Self::BackfillInProgress => \"Backfill In Progress\",\n            Self::Verifying => \"Verifying\",\n            Self::Swapped => \"Swapped\",\n            Self::CleaningUp => \"Cleaning Up\",\n            Self::Complete => \"Complete\",\n            Self::Failed => \"Failed\",\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse from u8 (for storage).\n    pub fn from_u8(v: u8) -> Option<Self> {\n        match v {\n            0 => Some(Self::Idle),\n            1 => Some(Self::ShadowCreated),\n            2 => Some(Self::DualWriteActive),\n            3 => Some(Self::BackfillInProgress),\n            4 => Some(Self::Verifying),\n            5 => Some(Self::Swapped),\n            6 => Some(Self::CleaningUp),\n            7 => Some(Self::Complete),\n            8 => Some(Self::Failed),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Active resharding operation state.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardOperation {\n    /// Unique operation ID.\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Index UID being resharded.\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    /// Old shard count.\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    /// New shard count.\n    pub target_shards: u32,\n    /// Current phase.\n    pub phase: ReshardPhase,\n    /// Phase started at (UNIX ms).\n    pub phase_started_at: u64,\n    /// Operation created at (UNIX ms).\n    pub created_at: u64,\n    /// Documents backfilled so far.\n    pub documents_backfilled: u64,\n    /// Total documents to backfill (estimated at start).\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// Last error message (if any).\n    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n    /// Shadow index UID.\n    pub shadow_index: String,\n    /// Verification results (populated after phase 4).\n    pub verification_results: Option<VerificationResults>,\n    /// Cleanup retention deadline (UNIX ms).\n    pub cleanup_deadline: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n/// Results from phase 4 verification.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct VerificationResults {\n    /// Live index PK set size.\n    pub live_pk_count: u64,\n    /// Shadow index PK set size.\n    pub shadow_pk_count: u64,\n    /// PKs only in live index.\n    pub live_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs only in shadow index.\n    pub shadow_only_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// PKs with content hash mismatch.\n    pub mismatched_pks: Vec<String>,\n    /// Whether verification passed.\n    pub passed: bool,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardOperation {\n    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn new(index_uid: String, old_shards: u32, target_shards: u32) -> Self {\n        let id = format!(\"reshard-{}-{}\", index_uid, uuid::Uuid::new_v4());\n        let shadow_index = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", index_uid, target_shards);\n        let now = millis_now();\n        Self {\n            id,\n            index_uid,\n            old_shards,\n            target_shards,\n            phase: ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            phase_started_at: now,\n            created_at: now,\n            documents_backfilled: 0,\n            total_documents: 0,\n            last_error: None,\n            shadow_index,\n            verification_results: None,\n            cleanup_deadline: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Transition to the next phase.\n    pub fn advance_phase(&mut self, new_phase: ReshardPhase) {\n        self.phase = new_phase;\n        self.phase_started_at = millis_now();\n    }\n\n    /// Record an error and mark as failed.\n    pub fn fail(&mut self, error: String) {\n        self.last_error = Some(error);\n        self.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n    }\n\n    /// Update backfill progress.\n    pub fn update_backfill_progress(&mut self, backfilled: u64, total: u64) {\n        self.documents_backfilled = backfilled;\n        self.total_documents = total;\n    }\n\n    /// Calculate backfill progress ratio (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub fn backfill_progress(&self) -> f64 {\n        if self.total_documents == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        (self.documents_backfilled as f64) / (self.total_documents as f64)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the operation is in a terminal state.\n    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(\n            self.phase,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete | ReshardPhase::Failed\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Get the shadow index name.\n    pub fn shadow_index_name(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.shadow_index\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current UNIX timestamp in milliseconds.\nfn millis_now() -> u64 {\n    SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as u64\n}\n\n/// In-memory registry of active resharding operations.\n///\n/// In production, this is persisted to the task store (SQLite/Redis).\n/// This in-memory version is for single-pod development.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct ReshardRegistry {\n    operations: HashMap<String, ReshardOperation>,\n    /// Index UID -> active operation ID (at most one per index).\n    index_ops: HashMap<String, String>,\n}\n\nimpl ReshardRegistry {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::default()\n    }\n\n    /// Register a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn register(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if let Some(existing_id) = self.index_ops.get(&op.index_uid) {\n            return Err(format!(\n                \"Resharding already in progress for index '{}': {}\",\n                op.index_uid, existing_id\n            ));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.insert(op.index_uid.clone(), op.id.clone());\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get an operation by ID.\n    pub fn get(&self, id: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get mutable reference for updates.\n    pub fn get_mut(&mut self, id: &str) -> Option<&mut ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.get_mut(id)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the active operation for an index (if any).\n    pub fn get_for_index(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Option<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.index_ops\n            .get(index_uid)\n            .and_then(|id| self.operations.get(id))\n    }\n\n    /// Update an operation.\n    pub fn update(&mut self, op: ReshardOperation) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if !self.operations.contains_key(&op.id) {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", op.id));\n        }\n        self.operations.insert(op.id.clone(), op);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Complete an operation and remove from active index tracking.\n    pub fn complete(&mut self, id: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n        let op = self.operations.get(id).ok_or_else(|| format!(\"Operation '{}' not found\", id))?;\n        if !op.is_terminal() {\n            return Err(format!(\"Operation '{}' is not in a terminal state\", id));\n        }\n        self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// List all operations.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Vec<&ReshardOperation> {\n        self.operations.values().collect()\n    }\n\n    /// Clean up completed operations older than the retention period.\n    pub fn prune_completed(&mut self, max_age_ms: u64) {\n        let now = millis_now();\n        let mut to_remove = Vec::new();\n        for (id, op) in &self.operations {\n            if op.is_terminal() && (now.saturating_sub(op.created_at) > max_age_ms) {\n                to_remove.push(id.clone());\n                self.index_ops.remove(&op.index_uid);\n            }\n        }\n        for id in to_remove {\n            self.operations.remove(&id);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests_reshard_execution {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_display_names() {\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Idle.name(), \"Idle\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress.name(), \"Backfill In Progress\");\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::Failed.name(), \"Failed\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn phase_roundtrip_u8() {\n        for phase in &[\n            ReshardPhase::Idle,\n            ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated,\n            ReshardPhase::DualWriteActive,\n            ReshardPhase::BackfillInProgress,\n            ReshardPhase::Verifying,\n            ReshardPhase::Swapped,\n            ReshardPhase::CleaningUp,\n            ReshardPhase::Complete,\n            ReshardPhase::Failed,\n        ] {\n            let v = *phase as u8;\n            assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(v), Some(*phase));\n        }\n        assert_eq!(ReshardPhase::from_u8(255), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_creation() {\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.index_uid, \"products\");\n        assert_eq!(op.old_shards, 64);\n        assert_eq!(op.target_shards, 128);\n        assert_eq!(op.shadow_index, \"products__reshard_128\");\n        assert_eq!(op.phase, ReshardPhase::ShadowCreated);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_backfill_progress() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.total_documents = 1000;\n        op.documents_backfilled = 500;\n        assert!((op.backfill_progress() - 0.5).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn operation_terminal_states() {\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        assert!(!op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Failed;\n        assert!(op.is_terminal());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_single_op_per_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op1 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        reg.register(op1).unwrap();\n        let op2 = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 128, 256);\n        assert!(reg.register(op2).is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_get_for_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        let retrieved = reg.get_for_index(\"products\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.id, id);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_complete_releases_index() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let op = ReshardOperation::new(\"products\".into(), 64, 128);\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_some());\n        let op = reg.get_mut(&id).unwrap();\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        reg.complete(&id).unwrap();\n        assert!(reg.get_for_index(\"products\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_prune_old_completed() {\n        let mut reg = ReshardRegistry::new();\n        let mut op = ReshardOperation::new(\"test\".into(), 16, 32);\n        op.phase = ReshardPhase::Complete;\n        op.created_at = millis_now().saturating_sub(100_000); // 100s ago\n        let id = op.id.clone();\n        reg.register(op).unwrap();\n        reg.prune_completed(50_000); // prune ops older than 50s\n        assert!(reg.get(&id).is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":528,"startLine":495,"totalLines":1022}}}

   }\n\n    /// Calculate the hedge deadline for a node.\n    pub async fn hedge_deadline(&self, node_id: &str) -> Option<Duration> {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let stats = self.node_stats.read().await;\n        let node_stats = stats.get(node_id)?;\n        let deadline_ms = (node_stats.p95_ms * self.config.p95_trigger_multiplier)\n            .max(self.config.min_trigger_ms as f64);\n        Some(Duration::from_millis(deadline_ms as u64))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the best alternative replica for hedging.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the node ID with the lowest score (EWMA of latency, in-flight, error rate).\n    pub async fn best_alternative(\n        &self,\n        primary_node: &str,\n        candidates: &[String],\n    ) -> Option<String> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let stats = self.node_stats.read().await;\n        let primary_stats = stats.get(primary_node);\n\n        // Score = α·latency + β·in_flight + γ·error_rate\n        const LATENCY_WEIGHT: f64 = 1.0;\n        const INFLIGHT_WEIGHT: f64 = 2.0;\n        const ERROR_WEIGHT: f64 = 10.0;\n\n        let mut best = None;\n        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n\n        for candidate in candidates {\n            if candidate == primary_node {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let candidate_stats = stats.get(candidate);\n            let score = if let Some(s) = candidate_stats {\n                LATENCY_WEIGHT * s.p95_ms\n                    + INFLIGHT_WEIGHT * s.in_flight as f64\n                    + ERROR_WEIGHT * s.error_rate * 1000.0\n            } else {\n                // Unknown node: use default score\n                LATENCY_WEIGHT * 100.0\n            };\n\n            if score < best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                best = Some(candidate.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        best\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we should hedge a request.\n    pub async fn should_hedge(&self, node_id: &str) -> bool {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let stats = self.node_stats.read().await;\n        if let Some(s) = stats.get(node_id) {\n            // Hedge if in-flight count is low (avoid thundering herd)\n            s.in_flight < 10\n        } else {\n            true\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for HedgingManager {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(HedgingConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use 1.2);\n        assert_eq!(config.min_trigger_ms, 15);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_hedges_per_query, 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_latency_stats_new() {\n        let stats = NodeLatencyStats::new(\"node-1\".into());\n        assert_eq!(stats.node_id, \"node-1\");\n        assert_eq!(stats.p95_ms, 100.0);\n        assert_eq!(stats.in_flight, 0);\n        assert_eq!(stats.error_rate, 0.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_latency_stats_update() {\n        let mut stats = NodeLatencyStats::new(\"node-1\".into());\n        stats.update_latency(150.0);\n        assert!(stats.p95_ms > 100.0 && stats.p95_ms < 150.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_latency_stats_error() {\n        let mut stats = NodeLatencyStats::new(\"node-1\".into());\n        stats.record_error();\n        assert!(stats.error_rate > 0.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_latency_in_flight() {\n        let mut stats 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_hedge_deadline() {\n        let config = HedgingConfig {\n            enabled: true,\n            p95_trigger_multiplier: 1.5,\n            min_trigger_ms: 20,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let manager = HedgingManager::new(config);\n\n        // Get stats for a node to initialize it\n        let _stats = manager.get_or_create_stats(\"node-1\").await;\n\n        let deadline = manager.hedge_deadline(\"node-1\").await;\n        assert!(deadline.is_some());\n        // Should be at least min_trigger_ms\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_a268211c5e8d44918aafbb07","type":"tool_result","content":"148\t        }\n149\t    }\n150\t\n151\t    /// Update with a new latency sample.\n152\t    fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) {\n153\t        // Convert half-life to alpha: alpha = 1 - exp(-ln(2) * dt / half_life)\n154\t        // For simplicity, we use a fixed alpha corresponding to the half-life.\n155\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf(1.0 / half_life_ms as f64);\n156\t        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * latency_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * self.latency_p95_ms;\n157\t    }\n158\t\n159\t    /// Record an error.\n160\t    fn record_error(&mut self, half_life_ms: u64) {\n161\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf(1.0 / half_life_ms as f64);\n162\t        self.error_rate = alpha + (1.0 - alpha) * self.error_rate;\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    /// Record a success.\n166\t    fn record_success(&mut self, half_life_ms: u64) {\n167\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf(1.0 / half_life_ms as f64);\n168\t        self.error_rate = (1.0 - alpha) * self.error_rate;\n169\t    }\n170\t\n171\t    /// Increment in-flight and round-robin counters.\n172\t    fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n173\t        self.in_flight += 1;\n174\t        self.rr_counter += 1;\n175\t    }\n176\t\n177\t    /// Decrement in-flight counter.\n178\t    fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n179\t        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n180\t    }\n181\t}\n182\t\n183\t/// Adaptive replica selector.\n184\tpub struct ReplicaSelector {\n185\t    /// Configuration.\n186\t    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n187\t    /// Per-node state.\n188\t    node_state: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, NodeState>>>,\n189\t    /// Global round-robin counter.\n190\t    global_rr_counter: Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64>,\n191\t}\n192\t\n193\timpl ReplicaSelector {\n194\t    /// Create a new replica selector.\n195\t    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n196\t        Self {\n197\t            config,\n198\t            node_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n199\t            global_rr_counter: Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0)),\n200\t        }\n201\t    }\n202\t\n203\t    /// Select the best replica from a list of candidates.\n204\t    ///\n205\t    /// Returns the node ID of the selected replica.\n206\t    pub async fn select_replica(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n207\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n208\t            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no candidates provided\".into()));\n209\t        }\n210\t\n211\t        match self.config.strategy {\n212\t            ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n213\t            ReplicaSelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates).await,\n214\t            ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates).await,\n215\t        }\n216\t    }\n217\t\n218\t    /// Adaptive selection: choose lowest-scoring replica.\n219\t    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n220\t        let state = self.node_state.read().await;\n221\t\n222\t        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n223\t        if rand::random::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon {\n224\t            if let Some(&random) = candidates.choose() {\n225\t                return Ok(random.clone());\n226\t            }\n227\t        }\n228\t\n229\t        // Exploitation: pick lowest score\n230\t        let mut best = None;\n231\t        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n232\t\n233\t        for node_id in candidates {\n234\t            let node_state = state.get(node_id);\n235\t            let score = if let Some(s) = node_state {\n236\t                ReplicaScore::calculate(\n237\t                    s.latency_p95_ms,\n238\t                    s.in_flight,\n239\t                    s.error_rate,\n240\t                    &self.config,\n241\t                )\n242\t            } else {\n243\t                // Unknown node: use default score\n244\t                ReplicaScore::calculate(100.0, 0, 0.0, &self.config)\n245\t            };\n246\t\n247\t            if score < best_score {\n248\t                best_score = score;\n249\t                best = Some(node_id.clone());\n250\t            }\n251\t        }\n252\t\n253\t        best.ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no replica selected\".into()))\n254\t    }\n255\t\n256\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n257\t    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n258\t        let index = self.global_rr_counter.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed)\n259\t            as usize % candidates.len();\n260\t        Ok(candidates[index].clone())\n261\t    }\n262\t\n263\t    /// Random selection.\n264\t    async fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n265\t        candidates\n266\t            .choose(&mut rand::thread_rng())\n267\t            .cloned()\n268\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no candidates\".into()))\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    /// Record a request starting (increment in-flight).\n272\t    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node_id: &str) {\n273\t        let mut state = self.node_state.write().await;\n274\t        let entry = state.entry(node_id.to_string()).or_insert_with(NodeState::new);\n275\t        entry.increment_in_flight();\n276\t    }\n277\t\n278\t    /// Record a request completion.\n279\t    pub async fn record_completion(\n280\t        &self,\n281\t        node_id: &str,\n282\t        latency_ms: f64,\n283\t        success: bool,\n284\t    ) {\n285\t        let mut state = self.node_state.write().await;\n286\t        let entry = state.entry(node_id.to_string()).or_insert_with(NodeState::new);\n287\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n288\t        entry.update_latency(latency_ms, self.config.ewma_half_life_ms);\n289\t        if success {\n290\t            entry.record_success(self.config.ewma_half_life_ms);\n291\t        } else {\n292\t            entry.record_error(self.config.ewma_half_life_ms);\n293\t        }\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    /// Get current scores for all nodes.\n297\t    pub async fn get_scores(&self, node_ids: &[String]) -> Vec<ReplicaScore> {\n298\t        let state = self.node_state.read().await;\n299\t        node_ids\n300\t            .iter()\n301\t            .map(|node_id| {\n302\t                if let Some(s) = state.get(node_id) {\n303\t                    ReplicaScore::new(\n304\t                        node_id.clone(),\n305\t                        s.latency_p95_ms,\n306\t                        s.in_flight,\n307\t                        s.error_rate,\n308\t                        &self.config,\n309\t                    )\n310\t                } else {\n311\t                    ReplicaScore::new(node_id.clone(), 100.0, 0, 0.0, &self.config)\n312\t                }\n313\t            })\n314\t            .collect()\n315\t    }\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\timpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n319\t    fn default() -> Self {\n320\t        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n321\t    }\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t#[cfg(test)]\n325\tmod tests {\n326\t    use super::*;\n327\t\n328\t    #[test]\n329\t    fn test_config_default() {\n330\t        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n331\t        assert!(matches!(config.strategy, ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Adaptive));\n332\t        assert_eq!(config.latency_weight, 1.0);\n333\t        assert_eq!(config.inflight_weight, 2.0);\n334\t        assert_eq!(config.error_weight, 10.0);\n335\t        assert_eq!(config.ewma_half_life_ms, 5000);\n336\t        assert_eq!(config.exploration_epsilon, 0.05);\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    #[test]\n340\t    fn test_replica_score_calculate() {\n341\t        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n342\t        let score = ReplicaScore::calculate(100.0, 5, 0.01, &config);\n343\t        assert_eq!(score, 100.0 + 2.0 * 5.0 + 10.0 * 0.01 * 1000.0);\n344\t    }\n345\t\n346\t    #[tokio::test]\n347\t    async fn test_select_empty_candidates() {\n348\t        let selector =
