       }\n    }\n\n    /// Update with a new latency sample.\n    fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) {\n        // Convert half-life to alpha: alpha = 1 - exp(-ln(2) * dt / half_life)\n        // For simplicity, we use a fixed alpha corresponding to the half-life.\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf(1.0 / half_life_ms as f64);\n        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * latency_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * self.latency_p95_ms;\n    }\n\n    /// Record an error.\n    fn record_error(&mut self, half_life_ms: u64) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf(1.0 / half_life_ms as f64);\n        self.error_rate = alpha + (1.0 - alpha) * self.error_rate;\n    }\n\n    /// Record a success.\n    fn record_success(&mut self, half_life_ms: u64) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf(1.0 / half_life_ms as f64);\n        self.error_rate = (1.0 - alpha) * self.error_rate;\n    }\n\n    /// Increment in-flight and round-robin counters.\n    fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight += 1;\n        self.rr_counter += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement in-flight counter.\n    fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Adaptive replica selector.\npub struct ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    /// Per-node state.\n    node_state: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, NodeState>>>,\n    /// Global round-robin counter.\n    global_rr_counter: Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64>,\n}\n\nimpl ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Create a new replica selector.\n    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            node_state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            global_rr_counter: Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Select the best replica from a list of candidates.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the node ID of the selected replica.\n    pub async fn select_replica(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no candidates provided\".into()));\n        }\n\n        match self.config.strategy {\n            ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n            ReplicaSelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates).await,\n            ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates).await,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Adaptive selection: choose lowest-scoring replica.\n    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n        let state = self.node_state.read().await;\n\n        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n        if rand::random::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon {\n            if let Some(&random) = candidates.choose() {\n                return Ok(random.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Exploitation: pick lowest score\n        let mut best = None;\n        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n\n        for node_id in candidates {\n            let node_state = state.get(node_id);\n            let score = if let Some(s) = node_state {\n                ReplicaScore::calculate(\n                    s.latency_p95_ms,\n                    s.in_flight,\n                    s.error_rate,\n                    &self.config,\n                )\n            } else {\n                // Unknown node: use default score\n                ReplicaScore::calculate(100.0, 0, 0.0, &self.config)\n            };\n\n            if score < best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                best = Some(node_id.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        best.ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no replica selected\".into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n        let index = self.global_rr_counter.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed)\n            as usize % candidates.len();\n        Ok(candidates[index].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Random selection.\n    async fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[String]) -> Result<String> {\n        candidates\n            .choose(&mut rand::thread_rng())\n            .cloned()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"no candidates\".into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Record a request starting (increment in-flight).\n    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node_id: &str) {\n        let mut state = self.node_state.write().await;\n        let entry = state.entry(node_id.to_string()).or_insert_with(NodeState::new);\n        entry.increment_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a request completion.\n    pub async fn record_completion(\n        &self,\n        node_id: &str,\n        latency_ms: f64,\n        success: bool,\n    ) {\n        let mut state = self.node_state.write().await;\n        let entry = state.entry(node_id.to_string()).or_insert_with(NodeState::new);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n        entry.update_latency(latency_ms, self.config.ewma_half_life_ms);\n        if success {\n            entry.record_success(self.config.ewma_half_life_ms);\n        } else {\n            entry.record_error(self.config.ewma_half_life_ms);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get current scores for all nodes.\n    pub async fn get_scores(&self, node_ids: &[String]) -> Vec<ReplicaScore> {\n        let state = self.node_state.read().await;\n        node_ids\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| {\n                if let Some(s) = state.get(node_id) {\n                    ReplicaScore::new(\n                        node_id.clone(),\n                        s.latency_p95_ms,\n                        s.in_flight,\n                        s.error_rate,\n                        &self.config,\n                    )\n                } else {\n                    ReplicaScore::new(node_id.clone(), 100.0, 0, 0.0, &self.config)\n                }\n            })\n            .collect()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n        assert!(matches!(config.strategy, ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Adaptive));\n        assert_eq!(config.latency_weight, 1.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.inflight_weight, 2.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.error_weight, 10.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.ewma_half_life_ms, 5000);\n        assert_eq!(config.exploration_epsilon, 0.05);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_replica_score_calculate() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n        let score = ReplicaScore::calculate(100.0, 5, 0.01, &config);\n        assert_eq!(score, 100.0 + 2.0 * 5.0 + 10.0 * 0.01 * 1000.0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_empty_candidates() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        assert!(selector.select_replica(&[]).await.is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_round_robin() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n            strategy: ReplicaSelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(config);\n        let candidates = vec![\"a\".into(), \"b\".into(), \"c\".into()];\n\n        // Should cycle through candidates\n        assert_eq!(selector.select_replica(&candidates).await.unwrap(), \"a\");\n        assert_eq!(selector.select_replica(&candidates).await.unwrap(), \"b\");\n        assert_eq!(selector.select_replica(&candidates).await.unwrap(), \"c\");\n        assert_eq!(selector.select_replica(&candidates).await.unwrap(), \"a\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_record_completion() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n\n        selector.record_request_start(\"node-1\").await;\n        selector.record_completion(\"node-1\", 150.0, true).await;\n\n        let scores = selector.get_scores(&[\"node-1\".into()]).await;\n        assert_eq!(scores.len(), 1);\n        // P95 should be updated towards 150\n        assert!(scores[0].latency_p95_ms > 90.0 && scores[0].latency_p95_ms < 150.0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_adaptive_selects_lowest_score() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n            strategy: ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Adaptive,\n            exploration_epsilon: 0.0, // Disable exploration for test\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(config);\n\n        // Record some latencies\n        selector.record_completion(\"node-1\", 200.0, true).await;\n        selector.record_completion(\"node-2\", 50.0, true).await;\n        selector.record_completion(\"node-3\", 100.0, true).await;\n\n        let candidates = vec![\"node-1\".into(), \"node-2\".into(), \"node-3\".into()];\n\n        // node-2 should be selected (lowest latency)\n        // We need to try multiple times due to randomness\n        let mut counts = HashMap::new();\n        for _ in 0..100 {\n            let selected = selector.select_replica(&candidates).await.unwrap();\n            *counts.entry(selected).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n\n        // node-2 should be selected most often\n        assert_eq!(counts.get(\"node-2\"), Some(&100));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_strategy_serialization() {\n        let strategy = ReplicaSelectionStrategy::Adaptive;\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&strategy).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(json, \"\\\"adaptive\\\"\");\n    }\n}\n","numLines":269,"startLine":148,"totalLines":416}}}

{"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"file_path\":\"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/query_planner.rs\",\"content\":\"//! Shard-aware query planner (plan §13.4).\\n//!\\n//! Parses search filters to narrow the shard set when PK-constrained patterns are detected.\\n\\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\\nuse crate::router::shard_for_key;\\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashSet};\\n\\n/// Query planner configuration.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct QueryPlannerConfig {\\n    /// Whether the planner is enabled.\\n    #[serde(default = \\\"default_true\\\")]\\n    pub enabled: bool,\\n    /// Maximum PK literals allowed in a narrowable filter.\\n    #[serde(default = \\\"default_max_pk_literals\\\")]\\n    pub max_pk_literals_narrowable: u32,\\n    /// Whether to log planned queries.\\n    #[serde(default)]\\n    pub log_plans: bool,\\n}\\n\\nfn default_true() -> bool {\\n    true\\n}\\nfn default_max_pk_literals() -> u32 {\\n    128\\n}\\n\\nimpl Default for QueryPlannerConfig {\\n    fn default() -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            enabled: true,\\n            max_pk_literals_narrowable: default_max_pk_literals(),\\n            log_plans: false,\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Parsed filter expression.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\\npub enum FilterExpression {\\n    /// Simple equality: `{pk} = \\\"literal\\\"`\\n    SingleEqual {\\n        field: String,\\n        value: String,\\n    },\\n    /// IN clause: `{pk} IN [\\\"a\\\",\\\"b\\\",\\\"c\\\"]`\\n    InList {\\n        field: String,\\n        values: Vec<String>,\\n    },\\n    /// AND of two expressions.\\n    And(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\\n    /// OR of two expressions (non-narrowable).\\n    Or(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\\n    /// NOT (non-narrowable).\\n    Not(Box<FilterExpression>),\\n    /// Unknown/complex filter.\\n    Unknown,\\n}\\n\\n/// Query plan result.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\\npub struct QueryPlan {\\n    /// Whether the query was narrowed.\\n    pub narrowed: bool,\\n    /// Reason for narrowing or non-narrowing.\\n    pub narrowing_reason: Option<String>,\\n    /// Target shard IDs (empty = all shards).\\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\\n    /// Estimated fanout size (number of nodes to query).\\n    pub fanout_size: usize,\\n    /// Original filter (if any).\\n    pub original_filter: Option<Value>,\\n}\\n\\nimpl QueryPlan {\\n    /// Create a full fanout plan (no narrowing).\\n    pub fn full_fanout(shard_count: u32, nodes_per_shard: usize) -> Self {\\n        Self {\\n            narrowed: false,\\n            narrowing_reason: None,\\n            target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\\n            fanout_size: shard_count as usize * nodes_per_shard,\\n            original_filter: None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Create a narrowed plan.\\n    pub fn narrowed(target_shards: Vec<u32>, nodes_per_shard: usize, reason: String) -> Self {\\n        let fanout_size = target_shards.len() * nodes_per_shard;\\n        Self {\\n            narrowed: true,\\n            narrowing_reason: Some(reason),\\n            target_shards,\\n            fanout_size,\\n            original_filter: None,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Get the narrowing ratio (0.0 = no narrowing, 1.0 = single shard).\\n    pub fn narrowing_ratio(&self, shard_count: u32) -> f64 {\\n        if shard_count == 0 {\\n            return 0.0;\\n        }\\n        1.0 - (self.target_shards.len() as f64 / shard_count as f64)\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Query planner — analyzes filters to determine targetable shards.\\npub struct QueryPlanner {\\n    config: QueryPlannerConfig,\\n}\\n\\nimpl QueryPlanner {\\n    /// Create a new query planner.\\n    pub fn new(config: QueryPlannerConfig) -> Self {\\n        Self { config }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Plan a query based on its filter and primary key field.\\n    ///\\n    /// Returns a plan indicating which shards should be queried.\\n    pub fn plan_query(\\n        &self,\\n        filter: Option<&Value>,\\n        primary_key: &str,\\n        shard_count: u32,\\n        nodes_per_shard: usize,\\n    ) -> QueryPlan {\\n        if !self.config.enabled {\\n            return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard);\\n        }\\n\\n        let filter = match filter {\\n            Some(f) => f,\\n            None => return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard),\\n        };\\n\\n        // Parse the filter\\n        let parsed = self.parse_filter(filter);\\n\\n        // Analyze for PK-constrained patterns\\n        match self.analyze_pk_constraint(&parsed, primary_key) {\\n            Some(PkConstraint::Single(value)) => {\\n                let shard_id = shard_for_key(&value, shard_count);\\n                QueryPlan::narrowed(\\n                    vec![shard_id],\\n                    nodes_per_shard,\\n                    format!(\\\"pk filter: {} = \\\\\\\"{}\\\\\\\"\\\", primary_key, value),\\n                )\\n            }\\n            Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(values)) if values.len() <= self.config.max_pk_literals_narrowable as usize => {\\n                let mut shards: BTreeSet<u32> = BTreeSet::new();\\n                for value in &values {\\n                    shards.insert(shard_for_key(value, shard_count));\\n                }\\n                QueryPlan::narrowed(\\n                    shards.into_iter().collect(),\\n                    nodes_per_shard,\\n                    format!(\\\"pk filter: {} IN [{} values]\\\", primary_key, values.len()),\\n                )\\n            }\\n            Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(_)) => {\\n                // Too many values for narrowing\\n                QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard)\\n            }\\n            None => {\\n                // No PK constraint detected\\n                QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard)\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Parse a Meilisearch filter expression.\\n    fn parse_filter(&self, filter: &Value) -> FilterExpression {\\n        match filter {\\n            // Simple string: \\\"field = value\\\"\\n            Value::String(s) => self.parse_filter_string(s),\\n            // Array: OR of multiple conditions\\n            Value::Array(arr) => {\\n                let mut parsed = arr.iter().map(|v| self.parse_filter(v));\\n                let mut result = parsed.next().unwrap_or(FilterExpression::Unknown);\\n                for expr in parsed {\\n                    result = FilterExpression::Or(Box::new(result), Box::new(expr));\\n                }\\n                result\\n            }\\n            // Nested filter\\n            Value::Object(_) => self.parse_filter_object(filter),\\n            _ => FilterExpression::Unknown,\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Parse a filter string (e.g., \\\"field = value\\\").\\n    fn parse_filter_string(&self, s: &str) -> FilterExpression {\\n        // Try to parse \\\"field = value\\\" or \\\"field IN [value1, value2]\\\"\\n        let s = s.trim();\\n\\n        // Check for IN\\n        if let Some(pos) = s.find(\\\" IN \\\") {\\n            let field = s[..pos].trim().to_string();\\n            let rest = s[pos + 4..].trim();\\n            if let Some(values) = self.parse_array_literal(rest) {\\n                return FilterExpression::InList { field, values };\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        // Check for equality\\n        if let Some(pos) = s.find('=') {\\n            let field = s[..pos].trim().to_string();\\n            let value_str = s[pos + 1..].trim();\\n            // Remove quotes\\n            let value = value_str.trim_matches('\\\"').trim_matches('\\\\'').to_string();\\n            return FilterExpression::SingleEqual { field, value };\\n        }\\n\\n        // Check for AND\\n        if let Some(pos) = s.find(\\\" AND \\\") {\\n            let left = self.parse_filter_string(&s[..pos]);\\n            let right = self.parse_filter_string(&s[pos + 5..]);\\n            return FilterExpression::And(Box::new(left), Box::new(right));\\n        }\\n\\n        FilterExpression::Unknown\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Parse an array literal from a filter string.\\n    fn parse_array_literal(&self, s: &str) -> Option<Vec<String>> {\\n        let s = s.trim();\\n        if !s.starts_with('[') || !s.ends_with(']') {\\n            return None;\\n        }\\n\\n        let inner = &s[1..s.len() - 1];\\n        if inner.is_empty() {\\n            return Some(Vec::new());\\n        }\\n\\n        let mut values = Vec::new();\\n        for part in inner.split(',') {\\n            let value = part.trim().trim_matches('\\\"').trim_matches('\\\\'').to_string();\\n            values.push(value);\\n        }\\n        Some(values)\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Parse a filter object (nested structure).\\n    fn parse_filter_object(&self, obj: &Value) -> FilterExpression {\\n        // Handle nested objects with AND/OR\\n        if let Some(arr) = obj.get(\\\"and\\\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\\n            let mut parsed = arr.iter().map(|v| self.parse_filter(v));\\n            let mut result = parsed.next().unwrap_or(FilterExpression::Unknown);\\n            for expr in parsed {\\n                result = FilterExpression::And(Box::new(result), Box::new(expr));\\n            }\\n            return result;\\n        }\\n\\n        if let Some(arr) = obj.get(\\\"or\\\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\\n            let mut parsed = arr.iter().map(|v| self.parse_filter(v));\\n            let mut result = parsed.next().unwrap_or(FilterExpression::Unknown);\\n            for expr in parsed {\\n                result = FilterExpression::Or(Box::new(result), Box::new(expr));\\n            }\\n            return result;\\n        }\\n\\n        if let Some(inner) = obj.get(\\\"not\\\") {\\n            let expr = self.parse_filter(inner);\\n            return FilterExpression::Not(Box::new(expr));\\n        }\\n\\n        FilterExpression::Unknown\\n    }\\n\\n    /// Analyze a filter expression for PK constraints.\\n    fn analyze_pk_constraint(&self, expr: &FilterExpression, primary_key: &str) -> Option<PkConstraint> {\\n        match expr {\\n            FilterExpression::SingleEqual { field, value } if field == primary_key => {\\n                Some(PkConstraint::Single(value.clone()))\\n            }\\n            FilterExpression::InList { field, values } if field == primary_key => {\\n                Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(values.clone()))\\n            }\\n            FilterExpression::And(left, right) => {\\n                // Check if either side has a PK constraint\\n                match (self.analyze_pk_constraint(left, primary_key), self.analyze_pk_constraint(right, primary_key)) {\\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Single(v)), None) |\\n                    (None, Some(PkConstraint::Single(v))) => Some(PkConstraint::Single(v)),\\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Single(v1)), Some(PkConstraint::Single(v2))) if v1 == v2 => {\\n                        Some(PkConstraint::Single(v1))\\n                    }\\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Single(_)), Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(mut vals))) |\\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(mut vals)), Some(PkConstraint::Single(_))) => {\\n                        // Single value narrows the multiple\\n                        // This is a simplification; in reality we'd need to check if single is in multiple\\n                        Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(vals))\\n                    }\\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(v1)), Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(v2))) => {\\n                        // Union of both value sets\\n                        let mut set: HashSet<_> = v1.into_iter().collect();\\n                        set.extend(v2);\\n                        Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(set.into_iter().collect()))\\n                    }\\n                    (Some(constraint), None) | (None, Some(constraint)) => Some(constraint),\\n                    (None, None) => None,\\n                }\\n            }\\n            FilterExpression::Or(_, _) => {\\n                // OR with non-PK branch makes it non-narrowable\\n                None\\n            }\\n            FilterExpression::Not(_) => {\\n                // Negation is non-narrowable\\n                None\\n            }\\n            FilterExpression::Unknown => None,\\n            FilterExpression::SingleEqual { .. } | FilterExpression::InList { .. } => {\\n                // PK constraint on different field\\n                None\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Primary key constraint extracted from a filter.\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\\nenum PkConstraint {\\n    /// Single PK value.\\n    Single(String),\\n    /// Multiple PK values.\\n    Multiple(Vec<String>),\\n}\\n\\nimpl Default for QueryPlanner {\\n    fn default() -> Self {\\n        Self::new(QueryPlannerConfig::default())\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n#[cfg(test)]\\nmod tests {\\n    use super::*;\\n\\n    #[test]\\n    fn test_config_default() {\\n        let config = QueryPlannerConfig::default();\\n        assert!(config.enabled);\\n {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260503202053f8cd82ec50cb4095","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_74c3ebbc729f43f7bab84cc1","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/query_planner.rs","content":"//! Shard-aware query planner (plan §13.4).\n//!\n//! Parses search filters to narrow the shard set when PK-constrained patterns are detected.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::router::shard_for_key;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashSet};\n\n/// Query planner configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct QueryPlannerConfig {\n    /// Whether the planner is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Maximum PK literals allowed in a narrowable filter.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_pk_literals\")]\n    pub max_pk_literals_narrowable: u32,\n    /// Whether to log planned queries.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub log_plans: bool,\n}\n\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_max_pk_literals() -> u32 {\n    128\n}\n\nimpl Default for QueryPlannerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            max_pk_literals_narrowable: default_max_pk_literals(),\n            log_plans: false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parsed filter expression.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum FilterExpression {\n    /// Simple equality: `{pk} = \"literal\"`\n    SingleEqual {\n        field: String,\n        value: String,\n    },\n    /// IN clause: `{pk} IN [\"a\",\"b\",\"c\"]`\n    InList {\n        field: String,\n        values: Vec<String>,\n    },\n    /// AND of two expressions.\n    And(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\n    /// OR of two expressions (non-narrowable).\n    Or(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\n    /// NOT (non-narrowable).\n    Not(Box<FilterExpression>),\n    /// Unknown/complex filter.\n    Unknown,\n}\n\n/// Query plan result.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct QueryPlan {\n    /// Whether the query was narrowed.\n    pub narrowed: bool,\n    /// Reason for narrowing or non-narrowing.\n    pub narrowing_reason: Option<String>,\n    /// Target shard IDs (empty = all shards).\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    /// Estimated fanout size (number of nodes to query).\n    pub fanout_size: usize,\n    /// Original filter (if any).\n    pub original_filter: Option<Value>,\n}\n\nimpl QueryPlan {\n    /// Create a full fanout plan (no narrowing).\n    pub fn full_fanout(shard_count: u32, nodes_per_shard: usize) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            narrowed: false,\n            narrowing_reason: None,\n            target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n            fanout_size: shard_count as usize * nodes_per_shard,\n            original_filter: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a narrowed plan.\n    pub fn narrowed(target_shards: Vec<u32>, nodes_per_shard: usize, reason: String) -> Self {\n        let fanout_size = target_shards.len() * nodes_per_shard;\n        Self {\n            narrowed: true,\n            narrowing_reason: Some(reason),\n            target_shards,\n            fanout_size,\n            original_filter: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the narrowing ratio (0.0 = no narrowing, 1.0 = single shard).\n    pub fn narrowing_ratio(&self, shard_count: u32) -> f64 {\n        if shard_count == 0 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n        1.0 - (self.target_shards.len() as f64 / shard_count as f64)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Query planner — analyzes filters to determine targetable shards.\npub struct QueryPlanner {\n    config: QueryPlannerConfig,\n}\n\nimpl QueryPlanner {\n    /// Create a new query planner.\n    pub fn new(config: QueryPlannerConfig) -> Self {\n        Self { config }\n    }\n\n    /// Plan a query based on its filter and primary key field.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a plan indicating which shards should be queried.\n    pub fn plan_query(\n        &self,\n        filter: Option<&Value>,\n        primary_key: &str,\n        shard_count: u32,\n        nodes_per_shard: usize,\n    ) -> QueryPlan {\n        if !self.config.enabled {\n            return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard);\n        }\n\n        let filter = match filter {\n            Some(f) => f,\n            None => return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard),\n        };\n\n        // Parse the filter\n        let parsed = self.parse_filter(filter);\n\n        // Analyze for PK-constrained patterns\n        match self.analyze_pk_constraint(&parsed, primary_key) {\n            Some(PkConstraint::Single(value)) => {\n                let shard_id = shard_for_key(&value, shard_count);\n                QueryPlan::narrowed(\n                    vec![shard_id],\n                    nodes_per_shard,\n                    format!(\"pk filter: {} = \\\"{}\\\"\", primary_key, value),\n                )\n            }\n            Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(values)) if values.len() <= self.config.max_pk_literals_narrowable as usize => {\n                let mut shards: BTreeSet<u32> = BTreeSet::new();\n                for value in &values {\n                    shards.insert(shard_for_key(value, shard_count));\n                }\n                QueryPlan::narrowed(\n                    shards.into_iter().collect(),\n                    nodes_per_shard,\n                    format!(\"pk filter: {} IN [{} values]\", primary_key, values.len()),\n                )\n            }\n            Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(_)) => {\n                // Too many values for narrowing\n                QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard)\n            }\n            None => {\n                // No PK constraint detected\n                QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count, nodes_per_shard)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a Meilisearch filter expression.\n    fn parse_filter(&self, filter: &Value) -> FilterExpression {\n        match filter {\n            // Simple string: \"field = value\"\n            Value::String(s) => self.parse_filter_string(s),\n            // Array: OR of multiple conditions\n            Value::Array(arr) => {\n                let mut parsed = arr.iter().map(|v| self.parse_filter(v));\n                let mut result = parsed.next().unwrap_or(FilterExpression::Unknown);\n                for expr in parsed {\n                    result = FilterExpression::Or(Box::new(result), Box::new(expr));\n                }\n                result\n            }\n            // Nested filter\n            Value::Object(_) => self.parse_filter_object(filter),\n            _ => FilterExpression::Unknown,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a filter string (e.g., \"field = value\").\n    fn parse_filter_string(&self, s: &str) -> FilterExpression {\n        // Try to parse \"field = value\" or \"field IN [value1, value2]\"\n        let s = s.trim();\n\n        // Check for IN\n        if let Some(pos) = s.find(\" IN \") {\n            let field = s[..pos].trim().to_string();\n            let rest = s[pos + 4..].trim();\n            if let Some(values) = self.parse_array_literal(rest) {\n                return FilterExpression::InList { field, values };\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check for equality\n        if let Some(pos) = s.find('=') {\n            let field = s[..pos].trim().to_string();\n            let value_str = s[pos + 1..].trim();\n            // Remove quotes\n            let value = value_str.trim_matches('\"').trim_matches('\\'').to_string();\n            return FilterExpression::SingleEqual { field, value };\n        }\n\n        // Check for AND\n        if let Some(pos) = s.find(\" AND \") {\n            let left = self.parse_filter_string(&s[..pos]);\n            let right = self.parse_filter_string(&s[pos + 5..]);\n            return FilterExpression::And(Box::new(left), Box::new(right));\n        }\n\n        FilterExpression::Unknown\n    }\n\n    /// Parse an array literal from a filter string.\n    fn parse_array_literal(&self, s: &str) -> Option<Vec<String>> {\n        let s = s.trim();\n        if !s.starts_with('[') || !s.ends_with(']') {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let inner = &s[1..s.len() - 1];\n        if inner.is_empty() {\n            return Some(Vec::new());\n        }\n\n        let mut values = Vec::new();\n        for part in inner.split(',') {\n            let value = part.trim().trim_matches('\"').trim_matches('\\'').to_string();\n            values.push(value);\n        }\n        Some(values)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a filter object (nested structure).\n    fn parse_filter_object(&self, obj: &Value) -> FilterExpression {\n        // Handle nested objects with AND/OR\n        if let Some(arr) = obj.get(\"and\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n            let mut parsed = arr.iter().map(|v| self.parse_filter(v));\n            let mut result = parsed.next().unwrap_or(FilterExpression::Unknown);\n            for expr in parsed {\n                result = FilterExpression::And(Box::new(result), Box::new(expr));\n            }\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(arr) = obj.get(\"or\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n            let mut parsed = arr.iter().map(|v| self.parse_filter(v));\n            let mut result = parsed.next().unwrap_or(FilterExpression::Unknown);\n            for expr in parsed {\n                result = FilterExpression::Or(Box::new(result), Box::new(expr));\n            }\n            return result;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(inner) = obj.get(\"not\") {\n            let expr = self.parse_filter(inner);\n            return FilterExpression::Not(Box::new(expr));\n        }\n\n        FilterExpression::Unknown\n    }\n\n    /// Analyze a filter expression for PK constraints.\n    fn analyze_pk_constraint(&self, expr: &FilterExpression, primary_key: &str) -> Option<PkConstraint> {\n        match expr {\n            FilterExpression::SingleEqual { field, value } if field == primary_key => {\n                Some(PkConstraint::Single(value.clone()))\n            }\n            FilterExpression::InList { field, values } if field == primary_key => {\n                Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(values.clone()))\n            }\n            FilterExpression::And(left, right) => {\n                // Check if either side has a PK constraint\n                match (self.analyze_pk_constraint(left, primary_key), self.analyze_pk_constraint(right, primary_key)) {\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Single(v)), None) |\n                    (None, Some(PkConstraint::Single(v))) => Some(PkConstraint::Single(v)),\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Single(v1)), Some(PkConstraint::Single(v2))) if v1 == v2 => {\n                        Some(PkConstraint::Single(v1))\n                    }\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Single(_)), Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(mut vals))) |\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(mut vals)), Some(PkConstraint::Single(_))) => {\n                        // Single value narrows the multiple\n                        // This is a simplification; in reality we'd need to check if single is in multiple\n                        Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(vals))\n                    }\n                    (Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(v1)), Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(v2))) => {\n                        // Union of both value sets\n                        let mut set: HashSet<_> = v1.into_iter().collect();\n                        set.extend(v2);\n                        Some(PkConstraint::Multiple(set.into_iter().collect()))\n                    }\n                    (Some(constraint), None) | (None, Some(constraint)) => Some(constraint),\n                    (None, None) => None,\n                }\n            }\n            FilterExpression::Or(_, _) => {\n                // OR with non-PK branch makes it non-narrowable\n                None\n            }\n            FilterExpression::Not(_) => {\n                // Negation is non-narrowable\n                None\n            }\n            FilterExpression::Unknown => None,\n            FilterExpression::SingleEqual { .. } | FilterExpression::InList { .. } => {\n                // PK constraint on different field\n                None\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Primary key constraint extracted from a filter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum PkConstraint {\n    /// Single PK value.\n    Single(String),\n    /// Multiple PK values.\n    Multiple(Vec<String>),\n}\n\nimpl Default for QueryPlanner {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(QueryPlannerConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = QueryPlannerConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_pk_literals_narrowable, 128);\n        assert!(!config.log_plans);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_no_filter() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let plan = planner.plan_query(None, \"id\", 64, 2);\n        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 64);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_single_pk_filter() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let filter = serde_json::json!(\"id = \\\"product_123\\\"\");\n        let plan = planner.plan_query(Some(&filter), \"id\", 64, 2);\n        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 1);\n        assert!(plan.narrowing_reason.unwrap().contains(\"product_123\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_in_list_filter() {\n        let planner = assert!(plan.narrowed);\n        assert!(plan.target_shards.len() <= 3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_and_with_pk() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let filter = serde_json::json!(\"id = \\\"xyz\\\" AND category = \\\"electronics\\\"\");\n        let plan = planner.plan_query(Some(&filter), \"id\", 64, 2);\n        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_or_non_narrowable() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let filter = serde_json::json!([\"id = assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_plan_wrong_field() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let filter = serde_json::json!(\"category = \\\"electronics\\\"\");\n        let plan = planner.plan_query(Some(&filter), \"id\", 64, 2);\n        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_narrowing_ratio() {\n        let plan Parses filter expressions to determine which shards can be excluded\n4\t//! from the search fan-out.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n7\tuse crate::router::shard_for_key;\n8\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n9\tuse std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashSet};\n10\t\n11\t/// Query planner configuration.\n12\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n13\tpub struct QueryPlannerConfig {\n14\t    /// Whether the planner is enabled.\n15\t    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n16\t    pub enabled: bool,\n17\t    /// Maximum PK literals allowed in a narrowable filter.\n18\t    #[serde(default = \"default_max_pk_literals\")]\n19\t    pub max_pk_literals_narrowable: usize,\n20\t    /// Log query plans.\n21\t    #[serde(default)]\n22\t    pub log_plans: bool,\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\tfn default_true() -> bool {\n26\t    true\n27\t}\n28\tfn default_max_pk_literals() -> usize {\n29\t    128\n30\t}\n31\t\n32\timpl Default for QueryPlannerConfig {\n33\t    fn default() -> Self {\n34\t        Self {\n35\t            enabled: true,\n36\t            max_pk_literals_narrowable: default_max_pk_literals(),\n37\t            log_plans: false,\n38\t        }\n39\t    }\n40\t}\n41\t\n42\t/// A query plan produced by the planner.\n43\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n44\tpub struct QueryPlan {\n45\t    /// Whether the query was narrowable.\n46\t    pub narrowed: bool,\n47\t    /// Reason for narrowing (if applicable).\n48\t    pub narrowing_reason: Option<String>,\n49\t    /// Target shard IDs (empty if not narrowed).\n50\t    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n51\t    /// Estimated fan-out size.\n52\t    pub fanout_size: usize,\n53\t    /// Narrowing ratio (target_shards / total_shards).\n54\t    pub narrowing_ratio: f64,\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\timpl QueryPlan {\n58\t    /// Create a non-narrowed plan (full fan-out).\n59\t    pub fn full_fanout(total_shards: u32) -> Self {\n60\t        Self {\n61\t            narrowed: false,\n62\t            narrowing_reason: None,\n63\t            target_shards: vec![],\n64\t            fanout_size: total_shards as usize,\n65\t            narrowing_ratio: 1.0,\n66\t        }\n67\t    }\n68\t\n69\t    /// Create a narrowed plan.\n70\t    pub fn narrowed(target_shards: Vec<u32>, total_shards: u32, reason: String) -> Self {\n71\t        let fanout_size = target_shards.len();\n72\t        let narrowing_ratio = if total_shards > 0 {\n73\t            fanout_size as f64 / total_shards as f64\n74\t        } else {\n75\t            1.0\n76\t        };\n77\t        Self {\n78\t            narrowed: true,\n79\t            narrowing_reason: Some(reason),\n80\t            target_shards,\n81\t            fanout_size,\n82\t            narrowing_ratio,\n83\t        }\n84\t    }\n85\t}\n86\t\n87\t/// Parsed filter expression.\n88\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\n89\tenum FilterExpression {\n90\t    /// Equality check: field = \"literal\"\n91\t    Equals { field: String, value: String },\n92\t    /// IN check: field IN [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n93\t    In { field: String, values: Vec<String> },\n94\t    /// AND of two expressions\n95\t    And(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\n96\t    /// OR of two expressions\n97\t    Or(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\n98\t    /// Not a narrowable expression\n99\t    Other,\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t/// Query planner for shard-aware optimization.\n103\tpub struct QueryPlanner {\n104\t    /// Configuration.\n105\t    config: QueryPlannerConfig,\n106\t    /// Primary key field name for each index.\n107\t    primary_keys: std::sync::Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, String>>>,\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\timpl QueryPlanner {\n111\t    /// Create a new query planner.\n112\t    pub fn new(config: QueryPlannerConfig) -> Self {\n113\t        Self {\n114\t            config,\n115\t            primary_keys: std::sync::Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n116\t        }\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Register the primary key field for an index.\n120\t    pub async fn register_primary_key(&self, index: &str, pk_field: &str) {\n121\t        let mut pks = self.primary_keys.write().await;\n122\t        pks.insert(index.to_string(), pk_field.to_string());\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    /// Get the primary key field for an index.\n126\t    async fn get_primary_key(&self, index: &str) -> Option<String> {\n127\t        let pks = self.primary_keys.read().await;\n128\t        pks.get(index).cloned()\n129\t    }\n130\t\n131\t    /// Plan a search query.\n132\t    ///\n133\t    /// Analyzes the filter to determine which shards can be targeted.\n134\t    /// Returns a plan with narrowed shard IDs if applicable.\n135\t    pub async fn plan_query(\n136\t        &self,\n137\t        index: &str,\n138\t        filter: Option<&str>,\n139\t        shard_count: u32,\n140\t    ) -> QueryPlan {\n141\t        if !self.config.enabled || filter.is_none() {\n142\t            return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count);\n143\t        }\n144\t\n145\t        let filter = filter.unwrap();\n146\t        let pk_field = match self.get_primary_key(index).await {\n147\t            Some(pk) => pk,\n148\t            None => return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count), // Unknown PK\n149\t        };\n150\t\n151\t        // Parse the filter\n152\t        let expr = match self.parse_filter(filter) {\n153\t            Ok(expr) => expr,\n154\t            Err(_) => return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count),\n155\t        };\n156\t\n157\t        // Analyze for narrowability\n158\t        let narrowable = self.is_narrowable(&expr, &pk_field, &[]);\n159\t\n160\t        match narrowable {\n161\t            Narrowable::Single(value) => {\n162\t                let shard_id = shard_for_key(&value, shard_count);\n163\t                QueryPlan::narrowed(\n164\t                    vec![shard_id],\n165\t                    shard_count,\n166\t                    format!(\"pk filter: {} = \\\"{}\\\"\", pk_field, value),\n167\t                )\n168\t            }\n169\t            Narrowable::Multiple(values) => {\n170\t                let mut shards = BTreeSet::new();\n171\t                for value in values {\n172\t                    let shard_id = shard_for_key(&value, shard_count);\n173\t                    shards.insert(shard_id);\n174\t                }\n175\t                let shard_vec: Vec<u32> = shards.into_iter().collect();\n176\t                QueryPlan::narrowed(\n177\t                    shard_vec,\n178\t                    shard_count,\n179\t                    format!(\"pk filter: {} IN [{} values]\", pk_field, shard_vec.len()),\n180\t                )\n181\t            }\n182\t            Narrowable::NotNarrowable(reason) => {\n183\t                if self.config.log_plans {\n184\t                    tracing::debug!(\"Query not narrowable: {}\", reason);\n185\t                }\n186\t                QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count)\n187\t            }\n188\t        }\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Parse a filter expression (simplified Meilisearch filter syntax).\n192\t    fn parse_filter(&self, filter: &str) -> Result<FilterExpression> {\n193\t        // Very simplified parser - handles basic cases:\n194\t        // - field = \"literal\"\n195\t        // - field IN [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n196\t        // - expr AND expr\n197\t\n198\t        // Try equality first\n199\t        if let Some(eq) = self.try_parse_equals(filter) {\n200\t            return Ok(eq);\n201\t        }\n202\t\n203\t        // Try IN\n204\t        if let Some(in_expr) = self.try_parse_in(filter) {\n205\t            return Ok(in_expr);\n206\t        }\n207\t\n208\t        // Try AND\n209\t        if let Some(and_expr) = self.try_parse_and(filter) {\n210\t            return Ok(and_expr);\n211\t        }\n212\t\n213\t        Ok(FilterExpression::Other)\n214\t    }\n215\t\n216\t    /// Try to parse an equality expression: field = \"literal\"\n217\t    fn try_parse_equals(&self, filter: &str) -> Option<FilterExpression> {\n218\t        let filter = filter.trim();\n219\t        let eq_pos = filter.find(\" = \")?;\n220\t        if eq_pos == 0 {\n221\t            return None;\n222\t        }\n223\t\n224\t        let field = filter[..eq_pos].trim().to_string();\n225\t        let value_part = &filter[eq_pos + 3..];\n226\t        let value = self.extract_string_literal(value_part)?;\n227\t\n228\t        Some(FilterExpression::Equals { field, value })\n229\t    }\n230\t\n231\t    /// Try to parse an IN expression: field IN [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n232\t    fn try_parse_in(&self, filter: &str) -> Option<FilterExpression> {\n233\t        let filter = filter.trim();\n234\t        let in_pos = filter.find(\" IN \")?;\n235\t        if in_pos == 0 {\n236\t            return None;\n237\t        }\n238\t\n239\t        let field = filter[..in_pos].trim().to_string();\n240\t        let value_part = &filter[in_pos + 4..].trim();\n241\t        let values = self.extract_string_array(value_part)?;\n242\t\n243\t        Some(FilterExpression::In { field, values })\n244\t    }\n245\t\n246\t    /// Try to parse an AND expression.\n247\t    fn try_parse_and(&self, filter: &str) -> Option<FilterExpression> {\n248\t        let (left, right) = filter.split_once(\" AND \")?;\n249\t        let left_expr = self.parse_filter(left).ok()?;\n250\t        let right_expr = self.parse_filter(right).ok()?;\n251\t\n252\t        Some(FilterExpression::And(\n253\t            Box::new(left_expr),\n254\t            Box::new(right_expr),\n255\t        ))\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    /// Extract a string literal: \"value\"\n259\t    fn extract_string_literal(&self, s: &str) -> Option<String> {\n260\t        let s = s.trim();\n261\t        if !s.starts_with('\"') || !s.ends_with('\"') {\n262\t            return None;\n263\t        }\n264\t        Some(s[1..s.len() - 1].to_string())\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Extract a string array: [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n268\t    fn extract_string_array(&self, s: &str) -> Option<Vec<String>> {\n269\t        let s = s.trim();\n270\t        if !s.starts_with('[') || !s.ends_with(']') {\n271\t            return None;\n272\t        }\n273\t\n274\t        let inner = &s[1..s.len() - 1];\n275\t        if inner.is_empty() {\n276\t            return Some(vec![]);\n277\t        }\n278\t\n279\t        let mut values = Vec::new();\n280\t        for part in inner.split(',') {\n281\t            let value = self.extract_string_literal(part)?;\n282\t            values.push(value);\n283\t        }\n284\t\n285\t        Some(values)\n286\t    }\n287\t\n288\t    /// Check if an expression is narrowable for a given primary key field.\n289\t    fn is_narrowable(\n290\t        &self,\n291\t        expr: &FilterExpression,\n292\t        pk_field: &str,\n293\t        mut seen_or: bool,\n294\t    ) -> Narrowable {\n295\t        match expr {\n296\t            FilterExpression::Equals { field, value } if field == pk_field => {\n297\t                if !seen_or {\n298\t                    Narrowable::Single(value.clone())\n299\t                } else {\n300\t                    Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"PK equality under OR\".into())\n301\t                }\n302\t            }\n303\t            FilterExpression::In { field, values } if field == pk_field => {\n304\t                if !seen_or && values.len() <= self.config.max_pk_literals_narrowable {\n305\t                    Narrowable::Multiple(values.clone())\n306\t                } else {\n307\t                    Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Too many PK literals or under OR\".into())\n308\t                }\n309\t            }\n310\t            FilterExpression::And(left, right) => {\n311\t                // AND can only narrow - check both sides\n312\t                match (\n313\t                    self.is_narrowable(left, pk_field, seen_or),\n314\t                    self.is_narrowable(right, pk_field, seen_or),\n315\t                ) {\n316\t                    (Narrowable::Single(v), _) | (_, Narrowable::Single(v)) => {\n317\t                        Narrowable::Single(v)\n318\t                    }\n319\t                    (Narrowable::Multiple(mut v1), Narrowable::Multiple(v2)) => {\n320\t                        v1.extend(v2);\n321\t                        Narrowable::Multiple(v1)\n322\t                    }\n323\t                    (Narrowable::Multiple(v), Narrowable::Single(s)) => {\n324\t                        let mut vals = v;\n325\t                        vals.push(s);\n326\t                        Narrowable::Multiple(vals)\n327\t                    }\n328\t                    (Narrowable::Single(s), Narrowable::Multiple(v)) => {\n329\t                        let mut vals = v;\n330\t                        vals.push(s);\n331\t                        Narrowable::Multiple(vals)\n332\t                    }\n333\t                    _ => Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Cannot narrow AND expression\".into()),\n334\t                }\n335\t            }\n336\t            FilterExpression::Or(_, _) => {\n337\t                // OR at top level with non-PK branches is not narrowable\n338\t                Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"OR expression at top level\".into())\n339\t            }\n340\t            FilterExpression::Equals { .. } | FilterExpression::In { .. } => {\n341\t                Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Non-PK field\".into())\n342\t            }\n343\t            FilterExpression::Other => {\n344\t                Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Complex expression\".into())\n345\t            }\n346\t        }\n347\t    }\n348\t}\n349\t\n350\t/// Result of narrowability analysis.\n351\tenum Narrowable {\n352\t    /// Narrowable to a single PK value.\n353\t    Single(String),\n354\t    /// Narrowable to multiple PK values.\n355\t    Multiple(Vec<String>),\n356\t    /// Not narrowable.\n357\t    NotNarrowable(String),\n358\t}\n359\t\n360\timpl Default for QueryPlanner {\n361\t    fn default() -> Self {\n362\t        Self::new(QueryPlannerConfig::default())\n363\t    }\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t#[cfg(test)]\n367\tmod tests {\n368\t    use super::*;\n369\t\n370\t    #[test]\n371\t    fn test_config_default() {\n372\t        let config = QueryPlannerConfig::default();\n373\t        assert!(config.enabled);\n374\t        assert_eq!(config.max_pk_literals_narrowable, 128);\n375\t    }\n376\t\n377\t    #[test]\n378\t    fn test_query_plan_full_fanout() {\n379\t        let plan = QueryPlan::full_fanout(64);\n380\t        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n381\t        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 0);\n382\t        assert_eq!(plan.fanout_size, 64);\n383\t        assert_eq!(plan.narrowing_ratio, 1.0);\n384\t    }\n385\t\n386\t    #[test]\n387\t    fn test_query_plan_narrowed() {\n388\t        let plan = QueryPlan::narrowed(vec![1, 2, 3], 64, \"test\".into());\n389\t        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n390\t        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards, vec![1, 2, 3]);\n391\t        assert_eq!(plan.fanout_size, 3);\n392\t        assert!((plan.narrowing_ratio - 0.046875).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n393\t    }\n394\t\n395\t    #[tokio::test]\n396\t    async fn test_plan_single_pk_filter() {\n397\t        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n398\t        planner.register_primary_key(\"products\", \"id\").await;\n399\t\n400\t        let plan = planner.plan_query(\"products\", Some(\"id = \\\"sku-123\\\"\"), 64).await;\n401\t        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n402\t        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 1);\n403\t        assert!(plan.narrowing_reason.unwrap().contains(\"pk filter\"));\n404\t    }\n405\t\n406\t    #[tokio::test]\n407\t    async fn test_plan_multiple_pk_filter() {\n408\t        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n409\t        planner.register_primary_key(\"products\", \"id\").await;\n410\t\n411\t        let plan = planner\n412\t            .plan_query(\n413\t                \"products\",\n414\t                Some(\"id IN different shards\n420\t        assert!(plan.target_shards.len() >= 1);\n421\t        assert!(plan.target_shards.len() <= 3);\n422\t    }\n423\t\n424\t    #[tokio::test]\n425\t    async fn test_plan_non_pk_filter() {\n426\t        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n427\t        planner.register_primary_key(\"products\", \"id\").await;\n428\t\n429\t        let plan = planner\n430\t            .plan_query(\"products\", Some(\"category = \\\"electronics\\\"\"), 64)\n431\t            .await;\n432\t        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n433\t        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 0);\n434\t    }\n435\t\n436\t    #[tokio::test]\n437\t    async fn test_plan_no_filter() {\n438\t        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n439\t        let plan =      let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n446\t        // No PK registered for this index\n447\t        let plan = planner\n448\t            .plan_query(\"unknown\", Some(\"id = \\\"test\\\"\"), assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_array(\"[]\"), Some(vec![]));\n468\t        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_array(\"[\\\"a\\\"\"), None);\n469\t    }\n470\t}\n471\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"a9bfd6d8-55f9-46e9-9e13-f6977bfd5fb8","uuid":"6ea88f2e-c5e8-41e1-9050-f7735ecd5cb4","timestamp":"2026-05-03T12:22:00.959Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/query_planner.rs","content":"//! Shard-aware query planner for PK-constrained searches (plan §13.4).\n//!\n//! Parses filter expressions to determine which shards can be excluded\n//! from the search fan-out.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::router::shard_for_key;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::{BTreeSet, HashSet};\n\n/// Query planner configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct QueryPlannerConfig {\n    /// Whether the planner is enabled.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_true\")]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Maximum PK literals allowed in a narrowable filter.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_pk_literals\")]\n    pub max_pk_literals_narrowable: usize,\n    /// Log query plans.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub log_plans: bool,\n}\n\nfn default_true() -> bool {\n    true\n}\nfn default_max_pk_literals() -> usize {\n    128\n}\n\nimpl Default for QueryPlannerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            max_pk_literals_narrowable: default_max_pk_literals(),\n            log_plans: false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A query plan produced by the planner.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct QueryPlan {\n    /// Whether the query was narrowable.\n    pub narrowed: bool,\n    /// Reason for narrowing (if applicable).\n    pub narrowing_reason: Option<String>,\n    /// Target shard IDs (empty if not narrowed).\n    pub target_shards: Vec<u32>,\n    /// Estimated fan-out size.\n    pub fanout_size: usize,\n    /// Narrowing ratio (target_shards / total_shards).\n    pub narrowing_ratio: f64,\n}\n\nimpl QueryPlan {\n    /// Create a non-narrowed plan (full fan-out).\n    pub fn full_fanout(total_shards: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            narrowed: false,\n            narrowing_reason: None,\n            target_shards: vec![],\n            fanout_size: total_shards as usize,\n            narrowing_ratio: 1.0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a narrowed plan.\n    pub fn narrowed(target_shards: Vec<u32>, total_shards: u32, reason: String) -> Self {\n        let fanout_size = target_shards.len();\n        let narrowing_ratio = if total_shards > 0 {\n            fanout_size as f64 / total_shards as f64\n        } else {\n            1.0\n        };\n        Self {\n            narrowed: true,\n            narrowing_reason: Some(reason),\n            target_shards,\n            fanout_size,\n            narrowing_ratio,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parsed filter expression.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\nenum FilterExpression {\n    /// Equality check: field = \"literal\"\n    Equals { field: String, value: String },\n    /// IN check: field IN [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n    In { field: String, values: Vec<String> },\n    /// AND of two expressions\n    And(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\n    /// OR of two expressions\n    Or(Box<FilterExpression>, Box<FilterExpression>),\n    /// Not a narrowable expression\n    Other,\n}\n\n/// Query planner for shard-aware optimization.\npub struct QueryPlanner {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: QueryPlannerConfig,\n    /// Primary key field name for each index.\n    primary_keys: std::sync::Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, String>>>,\n}\n\nimpl QueryPlanner {\n    /// Create a new query planner.\n    pub fn new(config: QueryPlannerConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            primary_keys: std::sync::Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Register the primary key field for an index.\n    pub async fn register_primary_key(&self, index: &str, pk_field: &str) {\n        let mut pks = self.primary_keys.write().await;\n        pks.insert(index.to_string(), pk_field.to_string());\n    }\n\n    /// Get the primary key field for an index.\n    async fn get_primary_key(&self, index: &str) -> Option<String> {\n        let pks = self.primary_keys.read().await;\n        pks.get(index).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Plan a search query.\n    ///\n    /// Analyzes the filter to determine which shards can be targeted.\n    /// Returns a plan with narrowed shard IDs if applicable.\n    pub async fn plan_query(\n        &self,\n        index: &str,\n        filter: Option<&str>,\n        shard_count: u32,\n    ) -> QueryPlan {\n        if !self.config.enabled || filter.is_none() {\n            return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count);\n        }\n\n        let filter = filter.unwrap();\n        let pk_field = match self.get_primary_key(index).await {\n            Some(pk) => pk,\n            None => return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count), // Unknown PK\n        };\n\n        // Parse the filter\n        let expr = match self.parse_filter(filter) {\n            Ok(expr) => expr,\n            Err(_) => return QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count),\n        };\n\n        // Analyze for narrowability\n        let narrowable = self.is_narrowable(&expr, &pk_field, &[]);\n\n        match narrowable {\n            Narrowable::Single(value) => {\n                let shard_id = shard_for_key(&value, shard_count);\n                QueryPlan::narrowed(\n                    vec![shard_id],\n                    shard_count,\n                    format!(\"pk filter: {} = \\\"{}\\\"\", pk_field, value),\n                )\n            }\n            Narrowable::Multiple(values) => {\n                let mut shards = BTreeSet::new();\n                for value in values {\n                    let shard_id = shard_for_key(&value, shard_count);\n                    shards.insert(shard_id);\n                }\n                let shard_vec: Vec<u32> = shards.into_iter().collect();\n                QueryPlan::narrowed(\n                    shard_vec,\n                    shard_count,\n                    format!(\"pk filter: {} IN [{} values]\", pk_field, shard_vec.len()),\n                )\n            }\n            Narrowable::NotNarrowable(reason) => {\n                if self.config.log_plans {\n                    tracing::debug!(\"Query not narrowable: {}\", reason);\n                }\n                QueryPlan::full_fanout(shard_count)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Parse a filter expression (simplified Meilisearch filter syntax).\n    fn parse_filter(&self, filter: &str) -> Result<FilterExpression> {\n        // Very simplified parser - handles basic cases:\n        // - field = \"literal\"\n        // - field IN [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n        // - expr AND expr\n\n        // Try equality first\n        if let Some(eq) = self.try_parse_equals(filter) {\n            return Ok(eq);\n        }\n\n        // Try IN\n        if let Some(in_expr) = self.try_parse_in(filter) {\n            return Ok(in_expr);\n        }\n\n        // Try AND\n        if let Some(and_expr) = self.try_parse_and(filter) {\n            return Ok(and_expr);\n        }\n\n        Ok(FilterExpression::Other)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to parse an equality expression: field = \"literal\"\n    fn try_parse_equals(&self, filter: &str) -> Option<FilterExpression> {\n        let filter = filter.trim();\n        let eq_pos = filter.find(\" = \")?;\n        if eq_pos == 0 {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let field = filter[..eq_pos].trim().to_string();\n        let value_part = &filter[eq_pos + 3..];\n        let value = self.extract_string_literal(value_part)?;\n\n        Some(FilterExpression::Equals { field, value })\n    }\n\n    /// Try to parse an IN expression: field IN [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n    fn try_parse_in(&self, filter: &str) -> Option<FilterExpression> {\n        let filter = filter.trim();\n        let in_pos = filter.find(\" IN \")?;\n        if in_pos == 0 {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let field = filter[..in_pos].trim().to_string();\n        let value_part = &filter[in_pos + 4..].trim();\n        let values = self.extract_string_array(value_part)?;\n\n        Some(FilterExpression::In { field, values })\n    }\n\n    /// Try to parse an AND expression.\n    fn try_parse_and(&self, filter: &str) -> Option<FilterExpression> {\n        let (left, right) = filter.split_once(\" AND \")?;\n        let left_expr = self.parse_filter(left).ok()?;\n        let right_expr = self.parse_filter(right).ok()?;\n\n        Some(FilterExpression::And(\n            Box::new(left_expr),\n            Box::new(right_expr),\n        ))\n    }\n\n    /// Extract a string literal: \"value\"\n    fn extract_string_literal(&self, s: &str) -> Option<String> {\n        let s = s.trim();\n        if !s.starts_with('\"') || !s.ends_with('\"') {\n            return None;\n        }\n        Some(s[1..s.len() - 1].to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Extract a string array: [\"a\", \"b\", ...]\n    fn extract_string_array(&self, s: &str) -> Option<Vec<String>> {\n        let s = s.trim();\n        if !s.starts_with('[') || !s.ends_with(']') {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let inner = &s[1..s.len() - 1];\n        if inner.is_empty() {\n            return Some(vec![]);\n        }\n\n        let mut values = Vec::new();\n        for part in inner.split(',') {\n            let value = self.extract_string_literal(part)?;\n            values.push(value);\n        }\n\n        Some(values)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if an expression is narrowable for a given primary key field.\n    fn is_narrowable(\n        &self,\n        expr: &FilterExpression,\n        pk_field: &str,\n        mut seen_or: bool,\n    ) -> Narrowable {\n        match expr {\n            FilterExpression::Equals { field, value } if field == pk_field => {\n                if !seen_or {\n                    Narrowable::Single(value.clone())\n                } else {\n                    Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"PK equality under OR\".into())\n                }\n            }\n            FilterExpression::In { field, values } if field == pk_field => {\n                if !seen_or && values.len() <= self.config.max_pk_literals_narrowable {\n                    Narrowable::Multiple(values.clone())\n                } else {\n                    Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Too many PK literals or under OR\".into())\n                }\n            }\n            FilterExpression::And(left, right) => {\n                // AND can only narrow - check both sides\n                match (\n                    self.is_narrowable(left, pk_field, seen_or),\n                    self.is_narrowable(right, pk_field, seen_or),\n                ) {\n                    (Narrowable::Single(v), _) | (_, Narrowable::Single(v)) => {\n                        Narrowable::Single(v)\n                    }\n                    (Narrowable::Multiple(mut v1), Narrowable::Multiple(v2)) => {\n                        v1.extend(v2);\n                        Narrowable::Multiple(v1)\n                    }\n                    (Narrowable::Multiple(v), Narrowable::Single(s)) => {\n                        let mut vals = v;\n                        vals.push(s);\n                        Narrowable::Multiple(vals)\n                    }\n                    (Narrowable::Single(s), Narrowable::Multiple(v)) => {\n                        let mut vals = v;\n                        vals.push(s);\n                        Narrowable::Multiple(vals)\n                    }\n                    _ => Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Cannot narrow AND expression\".into()),\n                }\n            }\n            FilterExpression::Or(_, _) => {\n                // OR at top level with non-PK branches is not narrowable\n                Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"OR expression at top level\".into())\n            }\n            FilterExpression::Equals { .. } | FilterExpression::In { .. } => {\n                Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Non-PK field\".into())\n            }\n            FilterExpression::Other => {\n                Narrowable::NotNarrowable(\"Complex expression\".into())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of narrowability analysis.\nenum Narrowable {\n    /// Narrowable to a single PK value.\n    Single(String),\n    /// Narrowable to multiple PK values.\n    Multiple(Vec<String>),\n    /// Not narrowable.\n    NotNarrowable(String),\n}\n\nimpl Default for QueryPlanner {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(QueryPlannerConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = QueryPlannerConfig::default();\n        assert!(config.enabled);\n        assert_eq!(config.max_pk_literals_narrowable, 128);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_plan_full_fanout() {\n        let plan = QueryPlan::full_fanout(64);\n        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 0);\n        assert_eq!(plan.fanout_size, 64);\n        assert_eq!(plan.narrowing_ratio, 1.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_query_plan_narrowed() {\n        let plan = QueryPlan::narrowed(vec![1, 2, 3], 64, \"test\".into());\n        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards, vec![1, 2, 3]);\n        assert_eq!(plan.fanout_size, 3);\n        assert!((plan.narrowing_ratio - 0.046875).abs() < f64::EPSILON);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_single_pk_filter() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        planner.register_primary_key(\"products\", \"id\").await;\n\n        let plan = planner.plan_query(\"products\", Some(\"id = \\\"sku-123\\\"\"), 64).await;\n        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 1);\n        assert!(plan.narrowing_reason.unwrap().contains(\"pk filter\"));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_multiple_pk_filter() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        planner.register_primary_key(\"products\", \"id\").await;\n\n        let plan = planner\n            .plan_query(\n                \"products\",\n                Some(\"id IN [\\\"sku-1\\\", \\\"sku-2\\\", \\\"sku-3\\\"]\"),\n                64,\n            )\n            .await;\n        assert!(plan.narrowed);\n        // All three might hash to different shards\n        assert!(plan.target_shards.len() >= 1);\n        assert!(plan.target_shards.len() <= 3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_non_pk_filter() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        planner.register_primary_key(\"products\", \"id\").await;\n\n        let plan = planner\n            .plan_query(\"products\", Some(\"category = \\\"electronics\\\"\"), 64)\n            .await;\n        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n        assert_eq!(plan.target_shards.len(), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_no_filter() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let plan = planner.plan_query(\"products\", None, 64).await;\n        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_plan_no_primary_key_registered() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        // No PK registered for this index\n        let plan = planner\n            .plan_query(\"unknown\", Some(\"id = \\\"test\\\"\"), 64)\n            .await;\n        assert!(!plan.narrowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_string_literal() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_literal(\"\\\"hello\\\"\"), Some(\"hello\".into()));\n        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_literal(\"\\\"hello world\\\"\"), Some(\"hello world\".into()));\n        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_literal(\"hello\"), None);\n        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_literal(\"\\\"hello\"), None);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_string_array() {\n        let planner = QueryPlanner::default();\n        let result = planner.extract_string_array(\"[\\\"a\\\", \\\"b\\\", \\\"c\\\"]\");\n        assert_eq!(result, Some(vec![\"a\".to_string(), \"b\".to_string(), \"c\".to_string()]));\n        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_array(\"[]\"), Some(vec![]));\n        assert_eq!(planner.extract_string_array(\"[\\\"a\\\"\"), None);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":471,"startLine":1,"totalLines":471}}}
